He says the above two lines in sort of loud whisper.] Creating a cylinder using the SketchyPhysics solids toolbar is an excellent way to learn about this powerful physics engine in SketchUp, which can help you create dynamic and realistic 3D models. This tutorial will provide you with clear, detailed steps and helpful tips along the way to ensure your success. Step 1: Open SketchUp and Create a New File Start by launching SketchUp and creating a new file (File > New). Choose either metric or imperial units based on your preference. Step 2: Set Up the SketchyPhysics Plugin To access the SketchyPhysics solids toolbar, first ensure that the plugin is installed and enabled. Go to Window > Preferences (or SketchUp > Preferences on Mac) and click on 'Extensions' on the left side. Look for 'SketchyPhysics' in the list, check the box next to it if it isn't already checked, then close the Preferences window. If you don't have the plugin installed, download it from the official website and install it before proceeding. Step 3: Access the SketchyPhysics Solids Toolbar Now let's bring up the SketchyPhysics solids toolbar. Right-click anywhere in the workspace and select 'SketchyPhysics Solids' from the context menu. The toolbar should now appear, docked at the bottom or right edge of the screen. Step 4: Draw a Circle (Base of the Cylinder) Select the 'Circle' tool from the basic drawing tools within SketchUp, found in the toolbar at the top of the screen. Click somewhere in the workspace to place the center point of the circle, then input the desired radius when prompted. Once satisfied, press Enter to finalize the circle creation. This will serve as the base of our cylinder. Step 5: Copy the Circle (Creating the Profile) With the circle still selected, press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C on Mac) to copy the circle. Then, move your cursor upward vertically while holding down the Ctrl (Option on Mac) key to maintain the same horizontal plane. Input the height value for the cylinder when asked, and press Enter to complete the action. You now have two circles stacked one above the other - the profile needed to create the cylinder. Step 6: Use the Follow Me Tool to Create the Cylinder Select the 'Follow Me' tool from the SketchyPhysics solids toolbar. With the tool active, click on the lower circle (base), then drag the mouse pointer over the upper circle. Release the mouse button to generate the cylindrical shape between both circles. Now you have created a simple cylinder using the SketchyPhysics solids toolbar! Key Tips & Guidelines * Make sure the SketchyPhysics plugin is always updated to its latest version to avoid compatibility issues and bugs. * Keep in mind that all measurements are given in the chosen unit system (metric or imperial), so choose wisely depending on your project requirements. * Be aware that complex shapes might require additional support structures like walls or planes to remain stable during simulations in SketchyPhysics. * Don't forget to group or component your objects after they're finished; this keeps everything organized and easier to manage later on. mm, he pushed his way out and he was a beautiful butterfly. That's the bathroom floor not the kitchen floor. Title: How to Get a Command Block in Minecraft Command blocks are unique blocks that can execute typed commands within the game. They're typically used by map makers and server administrators to automate complex tasks or create custom mechanics. However, they aren't available in survival mode; you need to enable cheats or be in creative mode to obtain them. Here's our step-by-step guide on getting a command block in Minecraft. **Step 1: Start a New Game or Enter an Existing World** Open up your Minecraft launcher and start a new game or join an existing world where you have permission to modify settings (if playing multiplayer). **Step 2: Enable Cheats** _Note: This step is only necessary if you started a new single-player game._ Pause the game and click 'Open to LAN'. Check the 'Allow Cheats' option, then hit 'Start LAN World'. Cheats must be enabled because command blocks cannot be obtained without them. ![Open to LAN](https://minecraft.gamepedia.com/media/minecraft.gamepedia.com/4/4c/Open_to_LAN_button.png?version=e30f8d6a7b59fcbf28fd08deeb3bcddc) **Key Tip**: Enabling cheats will prevent achievements from being earned in this session. **Step 3: Switch to Creative Mode** Press F3 + N to hide the coordinates overlay (optional), press F5 to switch to third person view (also optional), and finally press F3 + T to show the debug screen temporarily. Look at the gamemode value - should be 0 for Survival. Change it to 1 using the following command: `/gamemode c`. Press enter to run the command. Key Tip: You can also toggle between game modes using /gamemode s for Survival, /gamemode c for Creative, /gamemode a for Adventure, and /gamemode sp for Spectator. **Step 4: Gain Op Privileges (Multiplayer Only)** If you're playing multiplayer, ask the server owner or admin to promote you to operator status ("op"). Alternatively, type `/op YourUsername` in the chat window if you have the required permissions. Having op privileges allows you to use commands like giving yourself items including command blocks. **Step 5: Give Yourself a Command Block** To get a command block, open the chat window and type `/give @p minecraft:command_block`, then press enter. The '@p' symbol targets the nearest player, which should be you. If successful, you'll receive a message saying "You received [1] Command Block(s)". **Step 6: Place the Command Block** Select the newly acquired command block from your hotbar and place it down as you would with any other block. A user interface will appear when you right-click the block. **Additional Information**: By default, command blocks require redstone power to activate their commands. To change this behavior, adjust the conditional and always active options found under the Mindropper and Comparator sections respectively. With these steps followed correctly, you now possess a command block ready to carry out whatever instruction you program into it! Remember, great responsibility comes with such power...use wisely! Title: How to Fill Out a Junior Achievement Sales Order Book Introduction: Junior Achievement (JA) is an organization that provides educational programs to help young people develop financial literacy, work readiness, and entrepreneurial skills. The JA Sales Order Book is a tool used by students participating in JA's Company Program to manage their sales process. This tutorial will guide you through filling out a JA Sales Order Book accurately and effectively. Step 1: Familiarize Yourself with the Layout Before starting, take some time to understand the layout and structure of the JA Sales Order Book. It typically consists of several columns such as Date, Customer Name, Product Code, Description, Quantity, Unit Price, Total Amount, and Payment Terms. Each row represents one order from a customer. Key Tip: Keep your Sales Order Book neat and organized so information can be quickly accessed when needed. Step 2: Enter the Correct Date In the 'Date' column, write down the date when the sale was made or agreed upon. Recording accurate dates allows tracking of sales trends over time and facilitates timely invoicing and shipping. Guideline: Use the format DD-MM-YYYY to ensure consistency across all entries. Step 3: Record the Customer's Details Write the full name or company name of your customer under the 'Customer Name' column. Having clear records of who bought what product helps maintain strong relationships with customers and supports efficient communication. Step 4: Identify Sold Products Using Product Codes Enter the unique code assigned to the sold product into the 'Product Code' column. Utilizing these codes simplifies inventory management and eliminates potential errors due to miscommunication about products. Step 5: Describe Each Item Sold Briefly describe each item sold in the 'Description' column. Providing descriptions ensures both parties have a shared understanding of the transaction details and serves as a reference point if questions arise later. Step 6: Input the Number of Units Sold Record the quantity of units sold next to the corresponding entry under the 'Quantity' column. Accurate unit counting prevents misunderstandings regarding quantities delivered and assists in maintaining proper stock levels. Step 7: Specify Unit Prices Input the price per unit of the sold items in the 'Unit Price' column. By recording individual prices, businesses can calculate profit margins, track revenue streams, and make informed decisions based on pricing strategies. Step 8: Calculate Totals Multiply the number of units sold by their respective unit prices and enter the resulting total amounts in the 'Total Amount' column. Summarizing totals offers a quick overview of sales value and enables easy identification of high-value transactions. Step 9: State Payment Terms Specify the payment terms agreed upon between your business and the customer in the 'Payment Terms' column. Clearly defining payment expectations reduces confusion around billing cycles and fosters healthy cash flow. Common Payment Term Examples include: * Net 30: Full payment within 30 days from the invoice date * Due Upon Receipt: Payment required immediately after delivery * 50% Deposit / 50% Balance: Two payments - half upfront, half post-delivery Conclusion: By following this comprehensive tutorial, users should now feel confident in completing a Junior Achievement Sales Order Book correctly and efficiently. Proper usage of this tool contributes significantly to managing successful sales operations while nurturing essential professional skills among participants. Take the empty soda can and have an adult poke a hole in the side of the can near the bottom with a strong sharp pencil. 2. Ask your adult to make sure any sharp edges at the hole are pushed into the can so it's safe for you to put your finger on the hole. 3. Fill the can with water, holding your finger over the hole. Turn out the overhead lights and stand over a sink. 4. Put a flashlight over the top of the can, and take your finger off the hole. 5. Where the water stream lands, you'll see a beam of light. The light from the flashlight bends and follows the water out of the hole in the can. The inside of the can and the surface of the water stream act like mirrors to reflect the beam of light. The light bounces around inside the water, and follows it wherever it flows. Cleaning your room incrementally is an effective way to keep your space tidy without feeling overwhelmed by the task at hand. By breaking down the process into manageable steps, you can maintain a clean and organized living environment with ease. Here's our comprehensive guide: **Step 1: Set Clear Goals and Deadlines** Establish specific objectives regarding what areas need attention (e.g., decluttering closet, organizing bookshelves) and when you want them completed. This will help create structure around your cleaning routine and ensure progress towards maintaining a consistently clean room. *Key Tip:* Be realistic about timeframes; rushing through tasks may result in hasty decisions leading to more cluttered spaces later on. **Step 2: Declutter Regularly** Schedule regular sessions dedicated solely to removing unnecessary items from your room. Sort belongings into categories such as 'keep', 'donate', or 'throw away'. Keep only those things that are truly useful or meaningful to you. *Guideline:* Implement the "one-in-one-out" rule - whenever you bring something new into your room, remove an old item. **Step 3: Create Storage Solutions** Organize remaining possessions using appropriate storage solutions like boxes, bins, hangers, etc. Label containers clearly so you know exactly where everything belongs. Group similar items together which makes finding them easier. *Key Tip:* Use vertical space effectively by installing shelves or wall organizers if floor area is limited. **Step 4: Establish Daily Habits** Develop small daily habits that contribute significantly to overall room cleanliness overtime. These could include making your bed every morning, washing dishes immediately after use, placing dirty clothes directly into laundry hamper, etc. *Guideline:* Remember, consistency is crucial here. Even seemingly insignificant actions done regularly make a big difference. **Step 5: Allocate Time Weekly For Deep Cleansing** Once per week, dedicate extra time to deep clean certain spots often overlooked during daily routines. Dust off ceiling fans, wipe windowsills, vacuum under furniture, etc. *Key Tip:* Play some music or invite friends over afterwards to motivate yourself to complete these less exciting chores! **Step 6: Maintain Order After Completion** After completing all the above steps, continue to maintain order by dedicating just five minutes each day to ensuring everything remains neat and tidy. Putting things back where they belong right after usage prevents buildup of mess. *Guideline:* If you find yourself spending longer than five minutes, reevaluate your systems and see if there are ways to improve efficiency. By following this systematic approach, gradually but surely, your room will transform into a serene haven free of chaos and disorder. Happy cleaning! Do with your finger on the mirror how it goes. Introduction: Have you ever played with building blocks? You can stack them on top of each other to create all sorts of shapes and structures! In math, we sometimes use something called matrices, which are like boxes that hold numbers instead of blocks. Just like how you can arrange your building blocks in different ways to make new shapes, you can also do operations on matrices to get new ones. Today, we're going to talk about a special way to organize the numbers inside a matrix, called its "Jordan Decomposition." It's kind of like unstacking and rearranging your building blocks to see them more clearly! What is a Matrix?: Before we dive into Jordan Decomposition, let's first understand what a matrix is. Imagine having several groups of numbers, arranged neatly in rows and columns. We call this a matrix! Each number in it has its own spot, based on its row and column. The size of a matrix depends on how many rows and columns it has - just like how a box can have any number of blocks depending on its size. Matrices can represent systems of linear equations, transformations in geometry, or even play a role in solving puzzles! Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors: Now, let's learn two important concepts - eigenvalues and eigenvectors. When we multiply a matrix by another number (called a scalar), some special vectors don't change their direction; they simply stretch or shrink. These are our eigenvectors! The amount they stretch or shrink by is their corresponding eigenvalue. Every matrix has these unique pairs, telling us how it changes certain vectors when multiplied by itself. Jordan Decomposition: Finally, we arrive at Jordan Decomposition! Given a matrix A, we want to find a special 'change of basis' matrix P so that AP^-1 = P^{-1} J. This equation means that multiplying A by P gives the same result as multiplying P^-1 by J. The magic here lies within the matrix J - it looks almost like a diagonal matrix but has extra 1's along its superdiagonal. Once we reach this stage, working with J becomes easier since most entries become zero except those on the main diagonal and directly above. To better visualize this concept, think of moving around your building blocks until you find a perfect arrangement that helps you solve a puzzle easily. That's exactly what Jordan Decomposition does! In Conclusion: Understanding matrices and their various decompositions opens doors to numerous applications across science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. While complex ideas await high school and beyond, playing with basic matrices and learning about Jordan Decomposition lays a strong foundation. So next time you come across a problem involving matrices, remember that finding the best arrangement could lead you to a solution! Apparently the chicken was a big hit in the stuffed animal pile. Important parts of the North African Campaign of World War II were fought in Tunisia from 1941 to 1943. What d' you mean they are the same? Lisa didn't know if she wanted a Big Mac or a happy meal. Here are some things you can do to help your child learn effective problem-solving skills: - Help your child realize that it's up to her to solve problems. Don't jump in to solve a problem for your child if she quits. Don't say things like, "Here, let me do this for you. It's really too hard for someone your age." - Suggest different ways your child might solve a problem. For instance, if your child is having trouble putting a puzzle together by starting with the middle pieces, say, "What if you started with the corners?" - Boost your child's confidence that he is a good problem solver. When he shows you a completed project-even if it's a mud pie-praise how hard he worked. - Help your child know how good it feels to find a solution after "messing up" several times. When your child "messes up," encourage him to keep trying. Say, "good mistakes" are okay. They help us learn. Temperature is how hot or cold something is. Our bodies can feel the difference between something which is hot and something which is cold. To measure temperature more accurately, a thermometer can be used. Thermometers use a temperature scale to record how hot or cold something is. The scale used in most of the world is in degrees Celsius, sometimes called "centigrade". In the USA and some other countries and locations, degrees Fahrenheit are more often used while scientists mostly use kelvins to measure temperature because it never goes below zero. Adding affiliate links to your blog is an effective way to monetize your content while promoting products or services that you trust and believe will be beneficial to your audience. This tutorial will guide you through the process of adding affiliate links to your blog, with clear explanations, key tips, and best practices along the way. Step 1: Choose a Reliable Affiliate Program Before diving into adding affiliate links, first select a reputable affiliate program related to your niche. Some popular options include Amazon Associates, ShareASale, CJ Affiliate (formerly Commission Junction), Rakuten Marketing, and more. Research these programs thoroughly to ensure they align with your values and provide quality products relevant to your audience. Key tip: Sign up for multiple affiliate networks to diversify your income sources and maximize earning potential. Step 2: Create High-Quality Content Prioritize creating valuable content for your readers before incorporating affiliate links. Focus on providing solutions to their problems, answering questions, and engaging them in discussions within your chosen niche. By doing so, you establish credibility and build trust, increasing the likelihood of click-throughs and conversions when you introduce affiliate offers. Key guideline: Never compromise content quality by forcing irrelevant promotional material; this can damage reader relationships and harm your online reputation. Step 3: Identify Opportunities Within Existing Posts Review existing posts to identify opportunities where product recommendations make sense. For example, if you have written about 'top productivity tools,' consider including affiliate links to those resources. Be mindful of FTC disclosure requirements when integrating such links seamlessly within your text. Key tip: Use natural language when introducing affiliates; avoid sounding overly salesy or pushy as it may deter users from clicking. Step 4: Craft Effective Calls-to-Action (CTAs) Encourage user engagement using compelling CTAs alongside your affiliate links. Clearly communicate what action you want visitors to take next, whether subscribing to a newsletter, purchasing a recommended item, or signing up for a trial service. Experiment with various formats like buttons, banners, or simple text links. Key guideline: Make sure CTAs stand out visually without being intrusive, ensuring they blend well with overall design aesthetics. Step 5: Implement Tracking System To monitor performance metrics and optimize future strategies, implement tracking systems like UTM codes or dedicated link shorteners offered by affiliate platforms. These tools allow precise attribution, helping measure conversion rates and revenue generated per campaign. Key tip: Regularly analyze data insights to gauge success levels, fine-tune approaches, and discover emerging trends. Step 6: Comply With Legal Guidelines Adhere to Federal Trade Commission (FTC) regulations regarding full transparency around any financial relationship between yourself and affiliated brands. Place prominent disclosures atop every page containing affiliate links, informing readers that you earn commissions via qualifying purchases made through linked items. Key guideline: Maintain ethical business practices by following all applicable laws and guidelines to preserve integrity and foster positive public perception. By diligently applying these steps, you'll successfully integrate affiliate marketing efforts into your blog, generating additional revenue streams and enhancing visitor experiences concurrently. Remember always to prioritize quality content creation, maintain legal compliance, and experiment strategically to uncover optimal techniques tailored specifically to your unique readership demographics. One group was entirely outcrossing, the other was entirely selfing. The groups were subjected to a rugged terrain and repeatedly subjected to a mutagen. After 50 generations, the selfing population showed a substantial decline in fitness (= survival), whereas the outcrossing population showed no decline. This is one of a number of studies that show sexuality to have real advantages over non-sexual types of reproduction. This firebrat could go and put out the fire. As cathedrals are often particularly impressive buildings, the term is often used incorrectly to refer to any large important church. "Good luck! But Mr. Melrose is hard to move. Shelly the Shell at the Seashore Beginning Literacy Design By: Hannah Dupre Children's understanding of phonemes is vital to their survival as readers. Because of this necessity, we must teach children how to be phonemically aware. The problem is that children sometimes struggle to understand even the most basic phoneme concepts. One of the difficult concepts in phonemic awareness is the ability to understand digraphs. This lesson will specifically focus on the digraph sh=/sh/. It is important that students are able to recognize these two letters together and the sound that they make. In this lesson they will learn how to recognize sh=/sh/ by spelling and reading words with this correspondence. 1.Primary paper (1 per student) 2.Pencils (1 per student) 3.Chart with "She sells seashells by the seashore" 4.Class set of Elkonin boxes, one big set of Elkonin boxes and letters 5.Baggies with letters: sh, e, a, o, u, r, s, p, t 6.Chalk and chalkboard 7.Sheep on a Ship by Nancy E. Shaw 1.To begin the lesson I will talk about how we have already started learning about how one letter makes a certain sound, but now we are going to look at two specific letters that make a certain sound. I will tell them that when you put the s and h together, they will make the /sh/ sounds like in "shell." I will explain that we are going to become experts at spelling and reading the /sh/ sound in words. 2.Can anyone tell me what sound your mom or dad makes when they want you to quiet down? Your right! It is shh. Can we all practice saying shhh. Did you notice how your mouth moved to make that sound. Watch as I say shh again. You have probably also noticed that when someone says shh they usually put their index finger to their mouth. We are going to use this motion when we hear the /sh/ sound today. 3.Today we are going to try a tongue twister. There will be words in the tongue twister that make the sound /sh/, when you hear the sound I want you to put your index finger to your mouth like you would when you say shhh. Now I am going to read the tongue twister very slowly, "She sells seashells by the seashore." Good job! Will you say the tongue twister with me now? 4.Now, we are going to search to find the /sh/ in spoken words. I want you to tell me when you hear the sound. Do you hear it in shop or sip? Grape or shape? Push or pull? Shirts or pants? Shells or sand? 5.(Letterbox Lesson) Can everyone get their letterboxes out? I want you to fold them so that only three boxes are showing. I will pass out the baggies with only the letters they will need for the lesson. Next, I tell the students that I am going to say a few words and I want them to separate the words into the different sounds the makeup that word. First I will model by saying the word ship, then separating the words /sh/ /i/ /p/, and finally place the correct letters in the correct boxes. Did you notice how I had my s and h together in the same box? This is because they are one sound, which means they go together in one box. Now let's all try it! When I saw a word, I want you to put your letters in the right boxes according to the sounds in the word. I will say the words out loud to the children. The 3 phoneme words are shop, cash, dish and the 4 phoneme words are flesh. Now, since you all did such a wonderful job of spelling the words I want you to try to read the words. I will write the word on the board and I want you to read it aloud to me. I will model by writing the word shut on the board and then read it out loud. I will make sure all of the students wait a few seconds before blurting out the word so that everyone can have a chance to figure out the word. 6.Next, we are going to partner read Sheep on a Ship. I will introduce the book with a book talk. The book talk will be able the misadventures of a group of sheep that are stuck on a pirate ship. In order to find out if these sheep get away you will have to read the rest of the book. Once they are introduced to the book I will explain that they will take turns reading the book and when they come to a word with /sh/ in it I want them to write down the word. Once they have found all of the words in the book and both of them have read the book we will make a poster of all the words. 7.Finally, I will assess the students when they read new real words. The 3 phoneme words are ship, fish, sheep, shook, and shag. The 4 phoneme words are brush and flush. 7.Finally, I will assess the students when they read new real words. The 3 phoneme words are ship, fish, sheep, shook, and shag. The 4 phoneme words are brush and flush. Shaw, Nancy E. Sheep on a ship. Houghton Mifflin; Reprint edition 1992. Edema, Katie. Title: How to Change Your Name on Gaia Gaia is an online community where you can create your own avatar, play games, socialize with other members, and express yourself through various forms of content creation. If you want to change your name on Gaia for any reason, this tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step. ### Part 1 of 3: Logging into Gaia Online Step 1: Open your web browser To start changing your name on Gaia, open your preferred web browser such as Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Microsoft Edge, or Safari. This will allow you to access the Gaia Online website. Key Tip: Make sure that your web browser is up-to-date to ensure optimal performance when navigating the Gaia Online website. Step 2: Navigate to the Gaia Online homepage In the address bar of your web browser, type "[www.gaiaonline.com](http://www.gaiaonline.com)" and press Enter. This action will take you directly to the Gaia Online homepage. Guideline: If you are not already logged into your account, locate the 'Log In' button at the top right corner of the page and click on it. Then enter your username and password to proceed. ### Part 2 of 3: Accessing Account Settings Step 1: Locate your profile picture After logging into your Gaia account, look at the upper right corner of the screen. You should see your current profile picture along with some options like "Mail," "Notifications," and "Account." Step 2: Click on "Account" Move your cursor over the "Account" option, which should cause a dropdown menu to appear. From there, select "Settings" from the list. Step 3: Go to the Profile tab Once you have accessed your account settings, find the horizontal tabs near the top of the page labeled "Profile," "Preferences," "Security," etc. Select the first tab called "Profile." It contains all the necessary information related to your public persona on Gaia. ### Part 3 of 3: Changing Your Display Name Step 1: Find the display name field On the "Profile" tab, scroll down until you come across the section titled "Display Names & Titles." Within this area, you will find two input fields - one for your primary display name and another for your secondary display name (optional). Step 2: Edit your primary display name Click inside the text box next to "Primary Display Name" and delete your existing name. Type in the new name you wish to use on Gaia. Be mindful of naming policies while choosing your new name; avoid using offensive language, impersonation, or excessive punctuation. Key Tips: * Gaia allows users to change their names once every 60 days. Keep track of your last name change date if you plan to make frequent updates. * The maximum length for a display name is 45 characters. Step 3: Save changes After entering your desired new name, don't forget to save your changes by clicking the blue "Save Changes" button located below the input fields. A confirmation message will appear stating that your changes were saved successfully. Congratulations! You have now changed your name on Gaia. Remember to respect Gaia's terms of service and naming policies when selecting future usernames. Enjoy exploring the diverse world of Gaia with your updated identity! Once upon a time, there was a big fat fish named Bubbles. Bubbles lived in a pond with his fish friends. One day, Bubbles was feeling bored and wanted to do something fun. He asked his friend, "What job can we do today?" His friend replied, "Let's swim to the other side of the pond and see what we can find!" Bubbles and his friends swam to the other side of the pond. They found a big rock where they could rest. Suddenly, they heard a voice from above. It was a little girl who had come to the pond to play. She saw the fish and said, "Wow, you guys are so cool! I wish I could swim like you!" Bubbles replied, "Thanks! Swimming is our job. We love to swim and have fun in the water. You can swim too, just like us!" The little girl smiled and jumped into the pond. She swam with the fish and had a great time. From that day on, Bubbles and his friends had a new job - to teach others how to swim and have fun in the water. Introduce Vocabulary: Hot Air: The (Mostly) True Story of the First Hot Air-Balloon Ride (Priceman) What to Do Select three Tier Two vocabulary words to teach your students. A list of suggested words appears below. Write the vocabulary words on the board or on chart paper. 1. Introduce the story. Today we are going to read a story entitled Hot Air: The (Mostly) True Story of the First Hot Air-Balloon Ride. 2. Introduce the three vocabulary words you have chosen. Before we read the story, I want to introduce some new words that we will come across. Please repeat each word after I say it. 3. Read the story. Let's read the story. Make sure to listen for today's vocabulary words and to think about how they are used in the story. If you hear a vocabulary word while I am reading, raise your hand. 4. Define key vocabulary words. See definitions below. Let's think about our vocabulary words. The word ______________ means ____________. Does anyone remember how this word was used in the text? Call on students to answer the question. Then refer to the text to show how the word was used in context. Repeat this process for each vocabulary word. Now let's practice what we've learned. Amateur means someone who does a hobby for fun, not for money. What's the word? An amateur photographer took my pictures, but they were pretty good anyway. You're too young to be a professional race car driver; you're still an amateur. I'm going to name some activities. If you think the person doing the activity might be doing it just for fun, say amateur. Otherwise, stay quiet. Ready? - Brain surgery - Flying to the moon - Flying kites - Walking a dog - Growing flowers Assembled means built or put together. What's the word? My dad and I assembled a model car. Have you assembled your diorama for the science fair yet? I'm going to name some things. If you think the thing must be built or put together, say assembled. Otherwise, stay quiet. Ready? - A kite - A dog - A dog house - An aquarium Demonstration means showing a group of people how to do something. What's the word? We attended the cooking demonstration to learn how to make lasagna. Have you given a demonstration on how to take care of your horses? I'm going to name some things. If you think people need to be shown how to do the thing, say demonstration. Otherwise, stay quiet. Ready? - Use a chainsaw safely - Build a model rocket - Ride a horse Event means something that happens. What's the word? I went to an event at the fairgrounds last night. Do you remember the event we went to last week? I'm going to name some words. If you think the word means something that happens, say event. Otherwise, stay quiet. Ready? Experiment means a test to see if something will work. What's the word? It's a good thing Thomas Edison liked to do experiments; that's how he invented the light bulb. Did you read about Benjamin Franklin's experiment with the kite and key? I'm going to name some things. If you think someone might have done a test to see if the thing worked, say experiment. Otherwise, stay quiet. Ready? - The sun For Advanced Students: If time permits, have students create more examples for the vocabulary words. Title: How to Get Rid of Spider Webs Spiders can be beneficial as they help control pests; however, their webs can become an eyesore or even a nuisance when they appear in unwanted areas such as your home or garden. This tutorial will guide you through the process of getting rid of spider webs effectively and efficiently. It is important to note that this article focuses solely on removing existing webs and does not cover methods for preventing future web construction. **Safety First:** 1. Protective Gear: Before starting, ensure you have appropriate clothing including gloves (preferably rubber), long sleeves, pants tucked into socks, and closed-toe shoes. This protects against accidental contact with spiders which may lead to bites. 2. Allergy Alert: If you are allergic to spiders or suspect you might be, consider involving someone else in the task or seek medical advice before proceeding. **Tools Required:** * A broom or duster * Vacuum cleaner with hose attachment * Microfiber cloth or paper towels * Ladder if necessary * Optional - extendable dusters for high places **Step 1: Identify the Webs** Identifying where the spider webbings are located allows you to focus on those specific spots. Common locations include corners of rooms, ceilings, windowsills, outdoor eaves, porches, and gardens. **Step 2: Prepare Your Tools** Gather all necessary tools mentioned above. Having everything ready makes the process more efficient and less likely for any spiders to escape during preparation time. **Step 3: Start With a Broom or Duster** Using a broom or duster, gently knock down visible spider webs. Try to aim at capturing the spider along with the web. Dispose of them properly afterwards. Remember, some spiders can bite so handle carefully. Key Tip: Avoid using excessive force as this could result in eggs sacs being scattered around, potentially leading to increased spider population later. **Step 4: Use a Vacuum Cleaner** For remaining strands and debris, use a vacuum cleaner fitted with a hose attachment. The suction power should remove any residual threads and trapped insects within the web. Don't forget crevices and hard-to-reach spaces like behind furniture or beneath appliances. Guideline: After vacuuming, empty the bag or canister immediately to prevent any surviving spiders from escaping back into your space. **Step 5: Wipe Down Surfaces** Use a microfiber cloth or paper towel dampened slightly with water to wipe down surfaces where the webs were present. This ensures removal of leftover particles and discourages rebuilding in the same spot. Key Tip: For stubborn stains left by dried up webs, mix equal parts white vinegar and warm water then gently scrub the area until clean. **Step 6: Repeat Regularly** To maintain a web-free environment, regular cleaning is essential. Schedule weekly checks especially during seasons when spider activity is high. Remember, while this tutorial teaches you how to get rid of existing spider webs, it doesn't address prevention measures. To keep spiders away, declutter living spaces, seal entry points, reduce exterior lighting, and eliminate food sources like insects. By following these steps meticulously, you'll successfully eliminate spider webs from both indoor and outdoor environments. Stay safe, alert, and persistent, and soon enough, your space will be free from unsightly cobwebs! oh, okay you don't height eat it then. He made a sign of command to the unwilling Loki, and the latter cast the helmet on the heap. |Lesson 5 - Pronouns| A pronoun is a word we use to take the place of a noun, which can be a person, place or thing. We use it (pronoun) to avoid repeating a noun that had already been mentioned. We can write or say, "Jim has a bicycle. Jim rides his bicycle every day." But there's a better way of putting it using pronouns: Jim has a bicycle. He rides it every day. The words he and it are pronouns used to replace the nouns Jim and bicycle. Examples of pronoun are I, you, he, she, it, we, they, anyone, everyone, himself, myself, nobody, yourself, who. Other examples include this, that, all, any, each, none, some, that, what, which, etc. These pronouns can also be determiners, so how can we tell whether they are pronouns or determiners. It all depends on how they are used. As pronouns, they are used independently, that is without a noun following them. (See List 10 for indefinite pronouns) Through the back door who comes back? Skip to main content More Search Options A member of our team will call you back within one business day. The middle ear is an air-filled chamber that lies behind the eardrum. Pressure in the middle ear changes to match air pressure outside of the eardrum. When inside and outside pressures are balanced, the eardrum is flexible and normal hearing is more likely. Problems occur when air pressure in the middle ear drops. This is usually due to a block in the eustachian (u-STA-shun) tube, the narrow channel connecting the ear with the back of the throat. As the link between the middle ear and the throat, the eustachian tube has two roles. It helps drain normal, cleansing moisture from the middle ear. It also controls air pressure inside the middle ear chamber. When you swallow, the eustachian tube opens. This balances the air pressure in the middle ear with the pressure outside the eardrum. In infants and young children, the eustachian tube is short and almost level with the ear canal. By about age 7, however, the eustachian tube has become longer and steeper. This improves how well it works. The eardrum and middle ear are important to normal hearing. Together, they pass sound from the outer to the inner ear. When sound from the outer ear hits a flexible eardrum, the eardrum vibrates. The small bones in the middle ear pick up these vibrations and pass them along to the inner ear. There, the vibrations become electrical signals, which are sent along nerve pathways to the brain. So and she has a farm animal book that's similar to this it's. Title: How to Enable Push Notifications on iOS Introduction: Push notifications are alerts that appear on your iPhone, iPad, or iPod touch when you receive new messages, updates, or other information from apps. They help keep you informed about important events without requiring you to open the app constantly. This tutorial will guide you through enabling push notifications on your iOS device. Step 1: Unlock Your Device and Access Settings To begin, ensure your device is unlocked. Locate and press the 'Settings' icon (represented by gray gears) on one of your home screens or in the app library. This action opens the settings menu where you can customize various aspects of your device's functionality. Step 2: Navigate to the Notifications Section Scroll down until you find the 'Notifications' option (usually located towards the middle of the list). Tap on it to proceed. Here, you manage all notification-related settings across different applications installed on your device. Step 3: Review Overall Notification Settings At the top of the 'Notification Center', there are several switches controlling global notification behaviors such as 'Allow Notifications,' 'Sounds,' 'Badges,' and 'Show Previews.' These options determine whether any app can send notifications and if they should include sounds, badge app icons with numbers, or display previews of alert content. Make sure 'Allow Notifications' is turned ON if you want to enable push notifications. Key Tip: If you wish to have more control over individual app notifications but don't want to disable them entirely, consider turning off 'Sound' and 'Preview' features. This way, you still get visual cues for incoming notifications without interruptions like sound effects or revealing sensitive info accidentally. Step 4: Configure Individual App Notifications Below the global settings, you'll see a list of every application capable of sending push notifications. Each entry includes its current status regarding notifications, represented by a switch next to the app name. To modify an app's notification settings, tap its corresponding name. Step 5: Customizing Specific App Notifications Upon tapping an app name, you're taken to a dedicated page for managing that app's notification preferences. Like before, this screen has switches for toggling specific types of alerts, including Lock Screen, Notification Center, and Banners. You can also choose what kind of alert style banner appears - temporary ('Banners') or persistent ('Alerts'). Additionally, some apps offer further customization options like 'Sounds' and 'Show Previews'. Modify these according to your preference. Guideline: Remember, enabling too many push notifications may lead to excessive distractions throughout the day. Only allow notifications from essential apps or ones whose real-time updates matter most to you. Step 6: Save Changes and Test Notifications Once satisfied with your configuration choices, navigate back to the main 'Notifications' screen either via the '< Back' arrow at the upper left corner or by pressing the 'Notifications' label at the top of the screen. Any changes made take effect immediately. To test if everything works correctly, try triggering a push notification within an enabled app. For instance, send yourself a message using Messages or initiate a call via FaceTime. A pop-up banner should appear briefly on your screen indicating the received notification. Conclusion: Enabling push notifications on iOS allows users to stay updated on relevant information promptly. By following this comprehensive tutorial, you now know exactly how to configure these settings based on personal needs and priorities, enhancing productivity while minimizing unnecessary disturbances. To create Habitat, you will need: - Crayons or Markers - Glue Stick - Paper Plate or Piece of Cardboard Save it for later! Print it or put it on your e-reader.Download & Print Nests, Burrows, Houses, and Habitats Animals have homes just like people do! What are your child's favorite animals? Together, talk about those animals' habitats. Remind your child that a habitat is a place where animals live and can find food, water, and a place to sleep. What animal habitats might be near your home or in your local park? Look It Up Look in a book or online to learn about different animal habitats. How are the habitats different? For example, what is a tiger's habitat like? How is that different from a whale's habitat? Could a tiger live in a whale's habitat? Why not? Step 1: Things You'll Need Collect the items from the materials list. Little Hands Tip: Make as many animal sounds as you can. Play an animal sound guessing game. Step 2: Print It Out Print out the Habitat Sheet or help your child draw a habitat on a small piece of paper. Step 3: Home Decor If you've printed out the habitat picture, invite him to color and draw on it. What kind of animal lives here? Little Hands Tip: Talk about what will make his animal friend comfortable in its habitat. Step 4: Creating a Creature Ask him to draw a picture of the animal on another piece a paper. Step 5: Adults Do the Cutting Next, carefully cut out both pictures for your child. Step 6: Make the Circle Cut a paper plate or piece of thin cardboard into a circle. Step 7: Glue it Together Help him glue the habitat picture on one side and the animal picture on the other side. Step 8: From Pencil to Wand Attach an unsharpened pencil to the bottom of the plate with tape. Use enough tape so the plate is very secure. Step 9: Move It Encourage your child to hold the pencil between both palms. Then have him move one hand forward and the other hand backward, as though he is rubbing his hands together. The plate will spin quickly back and forth, and it will look like the animal is in its habitat! Little Hands Tip: Make up a story about a day in the life the animal. Step 11: Share It Make some more! What other animal and habitat combinations can you think of? You might try a frog in the wetlands, a camel in the desert, or a parrot in the rainforest! Evaluating success is an important process that can help individuals and organizations measure progress towards their goals and make informed decisions about future actions. This tutorial will provide a comprehensive guide on how to evaluate success, including detailed explanations, key tips, and guidelines. Step 1: Define Your Goals The first step in evaluating success is to clearly define your goals. Without specific, measurable goals, it's impossible to determine whether you've been successful. When defining your goals, be as specific as possible and include a deadline for completion. For example, instead of setting a goal to "increase sales," set a goal to "increase sales by 10% over the next six months." Key tip: Make sure your goals are SMART (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound) to ensure they are clear and actionable. Step 2: Identify Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Once you have defined your goals, the next step is to identify the KPIs that will allow you to measure progress toward those goals. KPIs are metrics that directly relate to your goals and provide a quantitative measure of success. For example, if your goal is to increase sales by 10% over the next six months, a relevant KPI might be the number of units sold or the total revenue generated. Guideline: Choose KPIs that are easy to track and interpret, and align with your overall business objectives. Step 3: Collect Data To evaluate success, you need data. Depending on your KPIs, this could involve tracking website traffic, monitoring social media engagement, conducting customer surveys, or analyzing financial reports. Use tools such as Google Analytics, social media analytics platforms, and CRM systems to collect accurate and reliable data. Key tip: Regularly schedule time to review and analyze your data to stay on top of trends and changes. Step 4: Analyze and Interpret Data After collecting data, the next step is to analyze and interpret it. Look for patterns, trends, and insights that can help you understand whether you are making progress toward your goals. Compare current data to historical data to see if there are any significant changes or deviations from expected results. Guideline: Be objective when interpreting data, avoiding confirmation bias or other cognitive biases that may influence your interpretation. Step 5: Draw Conclusions and Take Action Based on your analysis, draw conclusions about the effectiveness of your efforts and take appropriate action. If you are meeting or exceeding your goals, consider what strategies are working well and how you can build upon them. If you are falling short of your goals, identify areas where improvements can be made and develop a plan to address them. Key tip: Avoid making hasty decisions based solely on one data point; instead, look for consistent trends over time before taking action. Step 6: Communicate Results Finally, communicate your evaluation results to stakeholders, including team members, investors, and customers. Share both positive and negative findings, along with your plans for addressing any challenges or opportunities identified during the evaluation process. Guideline: Present data in a clear and concise manner using visualizations, charts, and graphs to facilitate understanding. By following these steps, you can effectively evaluate success and make informed decisions about future actions. Remember to regularly review and adjust your goals and KPIs as needed to ensure they remain aligned with your overall business objectives. With a disciplined approach to evaluating success, you can continuously improve performance, drive growth, and achieve your long-term vision. Elections are held every four years, and the winner of the election is the party or coalition of parties that gets the most votes and seats in the parliament. In this activity we're going to learn about different kinds of jobs and professions. What job or profession would you like? (answers will vary). Title: How to Create a Driveway Arch in The Sims 2 Creating a driveway arch in The Sims 2 can be a rewarding challenge that allows you to add a personal touch to your virtual homes. This guide will walk you through the process, offering detailed steps and helpful tips along the way. Please note that this task requires the purchase and installation of The Sims 2 and some expansion packs, as well as familiarity with the game's interface. ### Section 1: Gather Necessary Items Before starting, ensure you have access to the following items within your game: - A flat, open area where you wish to build the driveway arch - Adequate funds to purchase necessary materials - Building tools from either Base Game or an Expansion Pack (recommended: Open for Business) ### Section 2: Prepare the Ground 1. Enter Build Mode: Press 'B' to switch between Live and Build modes. You cannot modify terrain while in Live mode. 2. Flatten Terrain: Use the Terrain Tools ('T') to even out any bumps or irregularities in the ground where you plan to construct the arch. Smooth transitions are essential for a realistic appearance. 3. Level Foundation: After flattening the terrain, create a solid foundation using the Floor Tool ('F'). Choose a material that complements your home design. Remember that foundations may affect gameplay; consult online resources if needed. ### Section 3: Design the Layout 4. Draw the Outline: With the Wall Tool ('W'), draw two parallel lines representing the edges of the archway. Make sure they are symmetrical and spaced apart according to the width you desire for your driveway. 5. Adjust Height: Using the Elevation Tool (+/- keys), adjust the height of both walls so they meet at the top but leave enough space for vehicles to pass underneath. Keep in mind local building codes! 6. Add Columns: Place columns along the length of the archway for support. These can be purchased from the Buy Mode catalog under 'Building Structures > Decorative.' Select columns matching the architectural style of your house. 7. Connect Columns: Use the Fence Tool ('V') to connect the tops of the columns, creating a continuous line across the archway. This step is optional but adds realism and cohesion to the structure. ### Section 4: Customize Appearance 8. Apply Texture: From the Wall Patterns tab in Build Mode, choose a texture that matches your overall aesthetic. Apply this pattern consistently throughout the entire archway. 9. Paint Trim: Highlight the edge of the arch by applying a contrasting color or pattern to the trim. To do this, select the wall section just outside the archway and change its paint color accordingly. 10. Light Up the Night: Consider adding streetlights or other light sources near the entrance for visibility during nighttime play. Lights can be found in the 'Electronics' category under 'Lighting.' ### Key Tips and Guidelines - Save frequently to avoid losing progress due to unexpected crashes or errors. - Be aware of limitations regarding lot size, budget, and object placement when designing large structures like driveway arches. - Consult online communities and tutorials for additional ideas and inspiration. - Experiment with different styles and designs until you find one that suits your vision. Happy simming! Well, what does it say that fire truck. I better not, or I blow you up in the sky. Once upon a time, there was a little boy who decided to go for a walk. Participating in these communities can help you connect with other fans, learn more about The Watted, and even catch the attention of the band members themselves. This is a great way to show your support for the band and meet other fans in person. Take plenty of photos and videos at the event, and share them on Twitter. Be sure to tag The Wanted and use relevant hashtags. If the band members see your posts, they may be more inclined to follow you. **Step 6: Directly Contact The Wanted** While directly contacting The Wanted may seem intimidating, it can sometimes be effective. Send them a polite and respectful message expressing your admiration for their work and mentioning any notable contributions you've made to their fandom. However, do not spam them with multiple messages or demands for a follow back. Keep in mind that they are busy professionals and may not have time to respond to every message they receive. **Step 7: Stay Persistent but Patient** Getting The Wanted to follow you on Twitter may take time. Don't give up if you don't see results immediately. Continue engaging with their tweets, sharing relevant content, and participating in fan communities. Over time, your efforts are likely to pay off. Just remember to stay patient and persistent without being pushy or disrespectful. In common years, April starts on the same day of the week as October of the previous year, and in leap years, May of the previous year. In common years, April finishes on the same day of the week as July of the previous year, and in leap years, February and October of the previous year. In common years immediately after other common years, April starts on the same day of the week as January of the previous year, and in leap years and years immediately after that, April finishes on the same day of the week as January of the previous year. Step 1: Determine Why You Need an Indoor Car Cover Before you choose an indoor car cover, it's essential to understand why you need one. This will help you make an informed decision about the type of cover that best suits your needs. Here are some reasons why people use indoor car covers: * Protection from dust and dirt * Prevention of minor scratches and dings * Preservation of the vehicle's paint job * Shielding the interior from sun damage * Keeping the car hidden from view to deter theft Step 2: Measure Your Vehicle Accurately To ensure a proper fit, measure your car accurately before selecting a cover. Use a tape measure to record the length, width, and height of your vehicle. Be sure to include any mirrors or spoilers in your measurements. Having precise dimensions will help you select a cover that fits snugly without leaving gaps where debris can enter. Step 3: Choose the Right Material Indoor car covers come in various materials, each with its advantages and disadvantages. Some popular options include: * Polyester: Lightweight, breathable, and resistant to moisture. Ideal for short-term storage or protecting against light dust accumulation. * Cotton: Soft, comfortable, and non-abrasive. Great for preserving paint jobs and preventing minor scratches during long-term storage. * Satin: Smooth, sleek, and elegant. Provides excellent protection against dust, while also adding a touch of sophistication to your garage. * Flannel: Thick and warm. Suitable for cold climates where frost may be a concern. Step 4: Consider Additional Features When choosing an indoor car cover, consider additional features that might enhance its functionality: * Reinforced corners and seams: Added durability and resistance to tearing. * Elasticized bottom hem: Ensures a secure fit around the wheels and prevents slippage. * Grommets for locking: Allows you to secure the cover to the vehicle using a cable lock, providing extra security. * Breathability: Permits air circulation, reducing condensation buildup and mold growth. * Reflectivity: Helps maintain a consistent temperature inside the cover by reflecting sunlight. Step 5: Set a Budget Car covers range in price depending on their material, size, and features. Establishing a budget beforehand will narrow down your choices and prevent overspending. While it's important to invest in quality, remember that more expensive doesn't always mean better. Look for a balance between affordability and effectiveness. Step 6: Read Customer Reviews Customer reviews provide valuable insights into the performance and reliability of different indoor car covers. Pay attention to comments regarding fit, material quality, ease of use, and overall satisfaction. Avoid products with numerous negative reviews or recurring issues mentioned by multiple customers. Step 7: Purchase and Install Your Indoor Car Cover Once you've selected the perfect indoor car cover, place your order and wait for delivery. Upon arrival, carefully inspect the product for any defects or damages. If everything checks out, follow the manufacturer's instructions to install the cover correctly. Make adjustments as needed to ensure a snug fit that protects your vehicle from harm. By following these steps, you'll find the ideal indoor car cover for your automotive companion. Proper care and maintenance will preserve its appearance and extend its lifespan, ensuring many years of reliable service. Try not to be so rude about it please. [turns some pages back to show GAR] You will not be able to see the mouth, however, which is inside the shell. It is fitted with moving parts, and feelers, like the mouth of a crab. Also, the Barnacle has a good set of teeth to grind its food. It has no real eyes, having no use for them. Of what use are eyes to an animal standing on its head in a small dark shell! Now and then it casts its coat (like the Crab and Shrimp). The old coat is rolled up and thrown away outside the door. Now comes the strangest thing of all. As a baby, the Barnacle is a free swimming creature. It has three pairs of legs, a tail, a useful mouth, and one eye. After kicking about in the sea for some time, and changing its skin, it changes its shape entirely. It now looks more like a tiny mussel. It has two little "shells," two eyes, legs, and feelers. Now its swimming days are nearly over, and it must settle down. It gives up eating, and roves about looking and feeling for a place to settle on. Finding a suitable spot, the little animal stands on its head. Then a kind of glue is formed, which fixes it for life to that place, head down. The two shells and the two eyes are now thrown off. The Barnacle quickly builds up a shelly house, and, after a life of adventure and change, becomes a fixed Barnacle for the rest of its days. For many years people knew little of this strange animal. All its wonderful changes, and the way its body is made, tell us plainly that the Barnacle is actually first cousin to the Crab, Lobster, Shrimp and Prawn! It belongs to the class known as the Crustacea; but, for some reason or other, it has chosen to live its grown-up life fixed to a rock. 1. How does the Shrimp swim? 2. Of what use are Shrimps and Prawns in the sea? 3. How can you tell a live Shrimp from a live Prawn? 4. How does the Barnacle obtain its food? 5. Give the names of five crustaceans. PLANTS OF THE SHORE. To pick a bunch of gay flowers you would look in the fields and hedge-rows, and not by the sea. Flowers, as you know, love moist soil, and not dry sand; and, like us, they prefer one food to another. Sand they do not like, and salt is a poison to them. Both of these are enemies to plant life. Also, flowers choose sheltered spots. They do not like rough winds, and the glare of the sun shrivels them up. Yet there are plants with pretty flowers to be found by the sea, and many others with small, dull flowers. These seaside plants have to fight for their lives. The dry, shifting sand, and the salt spray, are enough to kill them, you would think. They have no shelter from the strong sea wind, nor from the fierce glare of the summer sun. The puzzle is, how do they live among so many enemies? For you know that the flowers of the field would at once die if you planted them in salt and sand. They would starve to death. It is not known exactly how this happened, but it could be that a piece of bread or grain became wet, and a short time later, it began to ferment and made a pulp that caused people to become drunk. A seal around 4,000 years old is a Sumerian "Hymn to Ninkasi", the goddess of brewing. This "hymn" is also a recipe for making beer. A description of the making of beer on this ancient engraving in the Sumerian language is the earliest account of what is easily recognised as barley, followed by a pictograph of bread being baked, crumbled into water to form a mash, and then made into a drink, that is recorded as having made people feel "...wonderful and blissful". It could even be possible that bread was first baked to be a way to make beer that is easy to carry around. The Sumerians were probably the first people to brew beer. Location: Setif, Algeria Positions: Classroom Teacher Administrator, English Teacher ic: Telling time using hand made clocks Grade Level: Fourth Materials: paper plates, paper fasteners, arrows made from black oak tag or cardboard strips 1. Using the materials listed above, have each student construct their own clock. Students may need help with the oak tag or heavy construction paper used to make the hands of the clock. 2. Discuss the best way to place the numbers on the clock so they will be arranged equal distance from each other. (Start with the 12, 6, 3, 9, then fill in the other numbers). 3. Have the students complete their clocks by filling in the marks for the minutes. There should be four marks between each number. Activity: Time can often be boring to learn, but tell students if they can tell time, they will know how much longer they will have to recess, lunch, and till the end of school. 1. Ask students to display the current time on their clocks. Show the time for lunch, recess, and the end of school on your clock. 2. Ask the students how many minutes there are until lunch? How many minutes there are until recess? How many hours and minutes until the end of school? 3. After the students share their answers and agree on one correct answer, they can test each other using their own clocks. For example, one child displays a time, and the other child makes up his own problem for the student to solve. They discuss their answers and agree on one. 4. Hiring an inheritance attorney is an important decision that can significantly impact the distribution of an estate after someone's death. This guide will walk you through the process of hiring an inheritance attorney, providing detailed explanations and key tips along the way. Step 1: Determine if You Need an Inheritance Attorney Before hiring an inheritance attorney, first assess whether or not your situation requires one. If there are disputes among heirs, complex assets involved, questions about the validity of the will, or concerns regarding tax implications, then consulting with an inheritance attorney would be beneficial. Key Tip: It's always better to consult early rather than waiting until issues arise as they may become more difficult and expensive to resolve later on. Step 2: Research Potential Attorneys Start by asking friends, family members, or colleagues for recommendations. Local bar associations also provide referral services where you can find attorneys specializing in probate law, which often includes inheritance matters. Once you have some names, research their credentials online. Look at reviews from past clients, check their standing with the state bar association, and review any disciplinary actions taken against them. Guideline: Narrow down your list based on location (preferably within your state), experience, client feedback, and cost-effectiveness. Step 3: Schedule Initial Consultations Most lawyers offer free initial consultations; take advantage of this opportunity to discuss your case and see if you feel comfortable working together. Prepare a list of questions beforehand so you don't forget anything crucial during the meeting. These might include: * Their experience handling similar cases * The estimated timeline for resolution * How fees are structured and billed * Who will handle your case primarily Remember, chemistry is essential when choosing a lawyer. Trust your instincts - if something feels off, keep looking. Step 4: Evaluate Compatibility and Expertise Pay attention to how well the attorney listens to your concerns and answers your questions. A good attorney should make you feel heard and understood while demonstrating expertise in inheritance laws applicable to your case. They should openly communicate potential strategies, risks, and costs associated with pursuing different options. Key Tip: Don't rush into decisions. Take time to reflect on each consultation and weigh the pros and cons carefully. Step 5: Understand Fees and Billing Practices Inheritance attorneys typically charge hourly rates but could also work on contingency basis (percentage of recovered funds) or flat fee arrangements depending upon the complexity of the matter. Make sure all terms are clearly outlined in writing including retainer amounts, billable hours, expenses reimbursement policies, etc. Guideline: Transparency about finances upfront prevents misunderstandings and surprises down the line. Step 6: Review Retainer Agreement Thoroughly Once you choose an attorney, you'll need to sign a retainer agreement outlining the scope of representation, responsibilities, and compensation. Read it thoroughly, ask questions if necessary, and ensure everything agreed upon verbally is reflected accurately. Key Tip: Never hesitate to negotiate unfavorable terms. Most attorneys are open to reasonable adjustments. Step 7: Maintain Open Communication Regular communication keeps both parties informed and aligned throughout the process. Share updates promptly, respond to messages promptly, and express any concerns directly. Remember, your attorney works for you and needs accurate information to represent you effectively. By following these steps, you increase chances of finding a competent, trustworthy inheritance attorney who can help navigate the intricacies of estate planning and distribution. Mount Arafat or Mount Arafah ( transliterated "Jabal 'Araf t") is a granite hill east of Mecca in the plain of Arafat. Arafat is a plain about southeast of Mecca. Mount Arafat reaches about in height and is also known as the Mount of Mercy ("Jabal ar-Rahmah"). Because the motto there are pals forever right? The Atlantic Ocean has important ocean currents. One of these, called the Gulf Stream, flows across the North Atlantic. Water gets heated by the sun in the Caribbean Sea and then moves northwest toward the North Pole. This makes France, the British Isles, Iceland, and Norway in Europe much warmer in winter than Newfoundland and Nova Scotia in Canada. Without the Gulf Stream, the climates of northeast Canada and northwest Europe might be the same, because these places are about the same distance from the North Pole. The formula for the volume of pyramids and cones tells you how much space is inside each object. For these two solid shapes, the volume formula is the same: it's one third of the area of the base times the height. Why? Here it is in a nutshell. The volume of three pyramids is equal to the volume of one prism with the same base and height. Similarly, the volume of three cones is equal to the volume of one cylinder with the same circular base and height. The volume of each cone is equal to Bh = (28.3 10) = 94 cm3. All three cones combined equals 283 cm3. The volume of the cylinder is equal to Bh = 28.3 10 = 283 cm3, ta da! The volume of each pyramid is equal to Bh = (18 8) = 48 cm3. All three pyramids combined equals 144 cm3. The volume of the prism is equal to Bh = 18 8 = 144 cm3. So he must have tops on it. What do you think he got out of those uh stuffed animals? When the cannonball is released it will move back and forth as diagrammed. It would do that forever except that the movement of the rope in the ring and rubbing in other places causes friction, and the friction takes away a little energy all the time. If we ignore the losses due to friction, then the energy provided by the horse is given to the cannonball as potential energy. (It has energy because it is up high and can fall down.) As the cannonball swings down it gains more and more speed, so the nearer the bottom it gets the faster it is going and the harder it would hit you if you stood in front of it. Then it slows down as its kinetic energy is changed back into potential energy. "Kinetic energy" just means the energy something has because it is moving. "Potential energy" just means the energy something has because it is in some higher position than something else. Once upon a time, there was a shy little duck. She lived in a pond with her friends. Watch out for that crazy Cat! Rationale: In order for beginning readers to become successful, it is important for students to be able to learn letter and sound correspondences. Short vowels are harder for students to learn and that is why it is vital that children are taught explicit instruction and practice with short vowels. The lesson will assist students in learning to recognize the correspondence a = /a/. The students will learn the correspondence a = /a/ by hearing and seeing the correspondence through spoken and written words. -Picture Cards (containing pictures of a cat, cow, bag, hat, dog, crab) -Letter boxes (enough for each student) -Letter tiles (enough for each student and including letters; c, a, t, g, r, b, l, d, s, p, n, k) -Copies of the text, A Cat Nap. (Enough for each student) -Overhead transparency of primary paper (for modeling the letter a) -Overhead transparency containing the tongue twister (Ally added apples and got a bad batch) -Letterbox for modeling on the overhead (for the teacher) -Letter tiles for modeling on the overhead (for the teacher) 1. Introduce the lesson to the students by explaining to them that each letter has a special sound and mouth movement. Tell the students that today, we will be learning the special sound and mouth movement for a = /a/. 2. I will start by asking the students, "What would you do if a crazy cat was chasing you?" Tell the students that they would probably scream "aaaaa!!" I will explain to the students that the letter /a/ makes the sound "aaaaa" just like if you were running from a crazy cat. I will tell the students that when we here the /a/ sound we are going to throw our hands up, just like we are scared. 3. I will tell the students that I have a very tricky tongue twister for them to say with the new "aaa" sound we have now learned. First, I am going to say it to you and I want you to listen for the "aaaa" sound we talked about. "Ally added apples and got a bad batch". I will ask the students if they were able to hear the sound that we had talked about. Then I will tell the students that we are going to say it together. "Ally added apples and got a bad batch". Good job! Now let's stretch out the /a/ sound. "Aaaaally aaaadded aaaapples aaaand got a baaaaad baaaaatch". Great job! 4. I will explain to the students, "Now, we are going use our letter boxes to spell some words using the /a/ sound that we have all learned. I want everyone to watch me closely as I spell the word glad". I will model on the overhead projector for the students how to spell the word glad using a letter box. I will also model how to say each sound as well. I will tell the students to watch as I spell another word, the word smash. I will use the overhead again to model using the vowel-coda-method to read the word. I will then pass out individual letter boxes and letter tiles to the students. I will ask the students to spell the letters that I say to them in their letterbox. I will remind the student that their boxes are for the sounds of the word not for the actual letters. I will give students an appropriate time limit in order to spell the words I call out. I will be walking around and observing the students work. If a student happens to spell a word incorrectly, I will say the word as they have spelled and not ask questions. After I have seen that the students were able to correctly spell the word, I will model it on the overhead. I will model each word after the students have correctly spelled it in order for them to see how the word is used correctly. The word list for the lesson is 3- [cat] 4 phonemes- [grab, crab, glad, slap, trap] 5- [blank]. 5. After I have had the students spell the word, I will have them read the words to me. I will use the overhead spell the words in order for the students to be able to see them. If I see that a student is having difficulty in spelling the words I will be sure to help the student in using body-coda to split the word. 6. I will introduce the students to a book called, A Cat Nap, which contains various words with the a = /a/ correspondence that the students have just learned. Each student will receive a copy of the text to read. I will introduce the book with a book talk about including the pictures. I will tell the students that the book is about a guy named Sam who has a cat named Tab. Tab likes to sleep in Sam's baseball bag. One day Sam doesn't know it but Tab is fast asleep in the baseball bag right before Sam plays. What do you think will happen? I will be observing the students as they read while I am walking around the room. 7. After reading the text, I will tell the students that it is time to write the new sound that we have just learned. In order to make the letter a, don't start at the fence. Start under the fence. Go up and touch the fence, then around and touch the sidewalk, around and straight down. 8. The students will receive picture cards with the picture of a cat, crab, bag, hat, cow, and dog. I will tell the students to look at the picture on the card. I will have the students identify each picture by writing underneath card what each picture is. I will be observing around the room while the students are working to see if they are getting the correct answers. I will assess the students by checking their work form their picture cards. I will focus on the letter that they have written in the word on the card. If I notice that a student has trouble with correctly writing the letter a or any problems with identifying the picture and the concept of the word containing the letter a, I will work with that student individually for help. The violin was not made directly from the instruments called viols. The word "violin" comes from the Middle Latin word "vitula". It means "stringed instrument". This word is also believed to be the source of the Germanic "fiddle". The modern European violin changed over time from many different bowed stringed instruments. They were brought from the Middle East and the Byzantine Empire. Most likely, the first makers of violins took ideas from three kinds of current instruments. They are the rebec, in use since the 10th century, the Renaissance fiddle, and the "lira da braccio." These instruments were held under the chin and bowed. Mosques, especially those in countries where Muslims are the majority, will also host "Eid" prayers outside in courtyards or town squares. For example, a person may measure the height of another person wearing shoes. The shoes make the height more than the same person without shoes. The caribou are now found mostly in North America, and they are larger than the reindeer. Large herds feed on the grass and lichens. When fall comes, they move south to winter feeding grounds. They dig through the snow with their sharp hoofs for food. Their hollow fur creates a cushion of air around them that helps them to stay warm. In the spring they return north to the summer pastures, where their calves are born. An hour or so after being born, the calves are able to follow the herd toward the Arctic Ocean. Most of the world's reindeer live in Europe and Asia. Some of these reindeer are not wild. They have been tamed by herdsmen who protect them from wolves and lead the migrations each spring and fall.
