Everyone is now on the plane, but people are still putting away their bags and other things. The flight attendant says: All baggage must be stowed in the overhead container. The word "baggage" means suitcases, bags, and other things that people take on trips. Airline and airport employees use the word "baggage". Passengers don't usually call it "baggage", though. They call it "luggage" or "bags" or "suitcases". This is a form that people use for giving formal instructions. It means that everyone has to do this. For example: The verb "stow" means "put". You "stow" something in a place when you want to keep or hide it. The word "stow" is especially used on boats and airplanes. A "container" is a thing that you put stuff in. These things are all "containers": - a box - a tub - a drawer - a jar An "overhead container" is the place on an airplane that people can put their suitcases and other baggage in. Title: How to Set Up and Use Block Proxy Servers Introduction: A block proxy server is an intermediary between a client (you) and any other servers. It allows you to access websites or services that may be blocked in your region by routing your connection through another country's server. This guide will walk you through setting up and using a block proxy server with clear instructions, detailed explanations, and helpful tips along the way. Step 1: Choose a Reliable Provider There are numerous free and paid block proxy service providers available online. Some popular ones include HideMyAss, NordVPN, ExpressVPN, CyberGhost, etc. Research various options based on factors such as speed, reliability, security features, customer support, pricing plans, and compatibility across devices. Select a provider that best suits your needs. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Read reviews from multiple sources before deciding. * Check if they keep logs and what kind of data they store. * Make sure they offer good encryption protocols. * Opt for trial versions whenever possible to test their services first-hand. Step 2: Sign Up for Their Service Once you have chosen a provider, sign up for their service either directly on their website or via an app downloaded onto your device. You typically need to provide basic information like email address and payment details (if applicable). Key Tips & Guidelines: * Always use a disposable email address for added privacy. * If choosing a monthly plan, set a reminder to cancel before auto-renewal if needed. Step 3: Download and Install Software/App Download the appropriate software or mobile application for your computer or handheld device. Follow installation prompts carefully until complete. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Ensure antivirus software doesn't flag the installer file as malicious. * During installation, opt out of additional toolbars or unwanted programs bundled with the main software. Step 4: Launch the Application and Connect Launch the newly installed program and enter your login credentials provided during registration. Choose a server location where the site/service isn't blocked and click 'Connect'. Wait for confirmation that the connection has been established successfully. Key Tips &Guidelines: * For optimal performance, choose servers closer to your actual geographical location. * Test connections during off-peak hours to avoid high user traffic affecting speeds. Step 5: Verify Connection Visit a website like ipcheck.info to verify whether your IP address reflects the one associated with the selected server. If correct, this confirms successful rerouting via the block proxy server. Key Tip: Bookmark this verification page for future quick checks. Step 6: Browse Restricted Content Now that you're connected, browse the previously restricted content without restrictions. Remember, while doing so, respect copyright laws and terms of usage. Additional Tips: * Regularly check for updates within the VPN software to maintain optimal functionality. * Consider multi-device subscriptions if planning to share the account among family members or different gadgets. * Contact customer support should issues arise during setup or operation. Conclusion: Using a block proxy server can open up a world of unrestricted browsing possibilities. By following these steps meticulously, users can enjoy secure, private, and unlimited internet access regardless of regional restrictions. Why doesn't the pack have liked mine right here? Age Range: Kindergarten through Grade 2 (Early Elementary) Overview and Purpose: In this lesson, students have to match the correct food to its place on the pyramid. Objective: The student will be able to match food to the correct category on the food pyramid. Pictures of different foods from all the food groups Hang the food pyramid on the wall of the classroom. Pass out a picture of a food from one of the food groups to each student. Have them come up one at a time and put their picture where it belongs on the pyramid. Talk about the pyramid. (Why there are more foods on the bottom than on the top? What are 'fats'?) Have students draw their own food pyramids using pictures of food that they like for each group. This activity can be kept in a center for students to practice with. Write the correct food group on the back of each food so students can check their work. Reading to your child helps them understand that stories have a beginning, middle and end. Telling stories helps children learn how to describe things, tell events in order and retell stories. Being able to talk about and explain what happens in a story helps a child understand the meaning of what he/she is reading. Good narrative skills lead to good reading comprehension. What can you do to help your child learn narrative skills? - Talk to your child. Talk about what you are doing, where you are going, or talk about your day. - Read to your child - Have your child tell you a story or retell a story - Sort items by shape, size or color let me see you get it, mister smiles. The next step is to "draw the temper" or "temper" the steel, so that it will not break like glass. To temper steel, a blacksmith polishes a part of the steel so that it is smooth and shiny. The blacksmith then slowly heats the steel in the fire. When the steel is between , the polished steel will turn different colors. These colors do not glow in the dark; they look like dye on the polished steel. As the steel gets hotter from 300 to 650 degrees F, it will turn through the colors: yellow, then brown, then purple, then blue. Yellow means the steel will still be harder, blue means the steel will be softer (but still hard). When the steel turns the color that the blacksmith wants, he puts the steel into a bucket of water to stop the change. Different tools are tempered to different colors, but it also depends on how much carbon there is in the steel. Usually, stone chisels are tempered to yellow, and axes for trees are tempered to blue, but the blacksmith has to decide. Some people eat honey that is still in the honeycomb. A worker bee grows wax in its abdomen. The wax appears as little spots. The bee pulls it off with its legs and chews it until it becomes soft. The wax is then used to make cells for the honeycomb. Smaller cells are used to raise worker bees. "sources of" vs. "the source of" Consider a scenario where a bloodstain was discovered and analyzed. It was determined the blood came from a single source. Joe is not the source of the blood. Jack is not the source of the blood. Which of the following statements is correct and why? Joe and Jack are excluded as SOURCES of the blood. Joe and Jack are excluded as THE SOURCE of the blood. Blue bear lived in a nice blue house and he drove a cute little blue car. Blue bear had many friends. They were blue bird, blue mouse, and blue fish. Every time blue bear picked a balloon, it was blue, and he only flew blue kites. Blue bear just loved (blue heart) blue. Everything in blue bear's world was blue. Not far away from blue bear lived yellow bear. Yellow bear lived in a very nice yellow house and he drove a cute little yellow car. Yellow bear had many friends. They were yellow bird, yellow mouse, and yellow fish. Every time yellow bear picked a balloon, it was yellow, and he only flew yellow kites. Yellow bear just loved (yellow heart) yellow. Everything in yellow bear's world was yellow. Down the road from yellow bear lived red bear. Red bear lived in a very nice red house and he drove a cute little red car. Blue bear had many friends. They were red bird, red mouse and red fish. Every time red bear picked a balloon it was red, and he only flew red kites. Red bear just loved (red heart) red. Everything in red bear's world was red. One summer day, blue bear was flying his blue kite with his friend blue bird. Blue bird was flying so high he could see the whole valley. From up in the sky blue bird saw yellow bear's house. He saw that yellow bear was have some trouble with his yellow car. Blue bird flew down and told blue bear all about poor yellow bears troubles. Blue bear did not know what to do. Blue bear never spoke to yellow bear before. He never played with yellow bear, he had never even been to yellow bears house. Blue fish said, "Yellow bear is a bear, and your are a bear. Maybe you are not so different." Blue bear knew that blue fish was right. He got in his blue car and drove to yellow bear's house. Blue bird followed. Yellow bear was surprised to see blue bear. Yellow bear did not know what to say. "Hello, I am blue bear, I live over there in the blue house. Title: How to Answer the Phone Only When You Are Home Alone Introduction: In today's world, where constant connectivity is the norm, answering phone calls has become an automatic response for many people. However, there might be situations where you prefer not to answer the phone when you are not alone at home. This could be due to various reasons such as privacy concerns or safety issues. Here is a comprehensive guide on how to answer the phone only when you are home alone. Step 1: Identify Your Reasoning Before implementing this practice, understand your motivations behind it. Knowing why you want to adopt this habit will help reinforce it over time. For instance, if your reason is to maintain privacy, reminding yourself of that can prevent any lapses in judgment. Key Tip: Be honest with yourself about your reasons so that they resonate deeply enough to create lasting change. Step 2: Recognize Caller ID Most modern phones come equipped with caller identification features. Familiarize yourself with this function if you aren't already comfortable using it. It allows you to see who's calling before you pick up. Guideline: If you don't recognize the number, let it go to voicemail. Check later when you are sure you're home alone. Step 3: Use Do Not Disturb Settings Modern smartphones have settings like 'Do Not Disturb' which silences all incoming calls unless specified otherwise (like contacts marked as favorites). Utilize these tools during times when you know you won't be alone. Additional Information: Some devices allow scheduling do-not-disturb periods, automating the process further. Step 4: Set Up Voicemail Greeting Customize your voicemail greeting to indicate that you may not always be available to take calls immediately. A polite yet clear message along these lines discourages hasty callers from persisting. Example: "Hello! Thank you for calling. I may not be able to get to the phone right away, but please leave me a message, and I'll return your call as soon as possible." Step 5: Develop Self-Discipline Resist the urge to impulsively answer every ring. Remember, maintaining control over your actions enhances both personal security and mental peace. Tips & Tricks: Try practicing mindfulness techniques to improve self-discipline. These practices teach you to stay focused on the present moment instead of reacting based on past habits. Step 6: Communicate With Regular Contacts Make those close to you aware of your new communication policy. Explain your reasoning calmly and respectfully. Most people should understand and accommodate your request. Dialogue Example: "Hey sis, just wanted to let you know that sometimes I might not answer my phone straight away, especially if I have company. But rest assured, I'll check messages regularly!" Conclusion: By following these steps, you can successfully manage your telephone etiquette even while balancing social interactions. Stay consistent, communicate openly, and utilize technological resources wisely - all crucial elements in mastering the art of answering the phone only when you're home alone. Let the Air out of the Balloon Rationale: It is important for children to understand that the letters in the alphabet phonemes that make up words. Before students can match the letters to their phoneme however they have to be recognize the phonemes in spoken words. This way, they understand the connection between the sound and written letter. This lesson is to help students learn the consonant "S" and the /s/ sound that it makes. Through the lesson, the students will recognize the phoneme in spoken words and then recognize the written that represents the sound. They will practice finding /s/ in words. Paper with, "Sssarah sssea while sssitting in the ssssand." a balloon with the letter "S" printed underneath it Siena at the Store; the sound of S by Cecilia Minden and Joanne D Meier Picture page with sun, bug, star, window, snake, flower, sword, table, chair, purse, sock, computer - Begin the lesson by explaining that our written language can be very tricky. "It is like a code that every one needs to learn to crack. Once you have cracked the code you are open to endless possibilities. You can understand symbols all around you. You can write messages to your friends and family and they will understand what you mean. Our mouths move when we say words and the sounds that come out represent letters which make up words. Today we are going to work on the sound /s/ and notice the way our mouth moves when we say it." - Ask students: "Have you ever heard the air come out of something? Like a balloon or a tire? It makes the /s/ sound. When you make the sound of air coming out of a balloon that is the /s/ mouth movement we are looking for. Let's pretend we see a balloon flying around that has the air coming out of it. Move your hand in the air and point to the imaginary balloon and let's make the /s/ sound together." [Point up in the air and make a figure 8 like you are trying to keep your finger on a moving balloon]. - "Now that we have learned the sound and mouth movement of /s/, everyone look up here at the board! Here is a tongue twister I want you to try (pass out sheet with tongue twister on it). Tongue twisters can be tricky, but they are also fun, Listen to me read it then we will say it together!" I say, "Sssarah ssscanned the sssea while sssitting in the ssssand. Let's read it together!" (We read it together.) "Now let's read it again, but this time slower and break the /s/ sound off the word: "/s/ arah /s/ canned the /s/ ea while /s/ itting in the /s/ - [Have students take out primary paper, pencils and pass out the picture of a balloon with air coming out and the letter "S" written under it.] "Remember how I told you that the sounds that come out of our mouths are represented by letters. The letter "S" is used to spell the /s/ sound. Let's practice writing the letter "S". Watch me write it on the board. You start halfway between the rooftop and the fence and curve up towards the roof. Once you get to the roof curve back to the fence and then curve down to the sidewalk. Once you get to the sidewalk curve back up and end in-between the sidewalk and the fence. It weaves around kind of like a snake! This is a capital "S" or a big "S". To make a lower-case "S" or a little "S" you only use the area from the fence to the sidewalk. Now I want everyone to try a capital "S" on your paper (repeat the directions). I am going to walk around and see snaky "S"s. When I put a sticker on your paper I want you to write nine more "S"s on your paper. Now when you see the letter "S" in a word, you know that it will make the /s/ sound". - "Now I am going to show you how to find /s/ in words. Listen to the word basket, I'm going to stretch out the word for you and I want you to listen for /s/ like the balloon that is loosing air! B-b- b- a- a- S- S- S . . . . * Do you hear it? There is the /s/! There is the air blowing out of the balloon in basket!" - Call on different students to tell what they think is the answer and how they knew: "Do you hear the /s/ in say or cut? Glass or bowl? Basement or attic? Desk or table? Snake or bear?" [Tell the students to use their balloon picture] Hold up your balloon if you hear the /s/ sound in any of the words I'm about to say. Sam, packed, his, suitcase, to, ride, a, bus, to, Seattle, to, see, Aunt, - Say: "Siena and her mother go to the store to buy sandals. When they get there they end up buying a lot more! Sure thing! Let me try my best to simplify this mathematical concept so that it can be understood by grade-school students. Have you ever played with pattern blocks before? They are colorful geometric shapes that you can arrange in different ways to create patterns or designs. One of the shapes is a hexagon, which looks like a six-pointed star. Imagine if we had a bunch of these hexagons, and we wanted to stack them on top of each other to build a tall tower. Now, let's say we want to know how high our tower will be if we add more and more hexagons. To do this, we need to find out the height of each individual hexagon first. But instead of measuring the height directly, we'll use something called "fractional parts" to make things easier. The fractional part of a number is just the decimal part of the number, without the whole number part. For example, the fractional part of 1.75 is 0.75, because we can write 1.75 as 1 + 0.75, where 1 is the whole number part and 0.75 is the decimal part. Similarly, the fractional part of 2.3 is 0.3, and the fractional part of 5 is 0 (because there is no decimal part). So, for each hexagon, we're going to measure its height using its fractional part. This may sound strange, but it turns out that doing this will give us some interesting patterns that we can explore! To start, let's define a new term called "Bernoulli polynomial". These are special functions that have some cool properties when you plug in fractional values. Don't worry too much about the details of these functions, but just remember that they exist and that they can help us describe the heights of our hexagons. Next, we're going to define a sequence of numbers $u\_n$, which represent the total height of all the hexagons up to the nth one. We calculate $u\_n$ by adding up the Bernoulli polynomials evaluated at the fractional parts of the reciprocals of the integers from 1 to n. In math language, we would write this as: $$u\_n = sum\_{i=1}^n frac{1}{i} B\_{2i+1}({"frac{1}{i}"})$$ Don't worry if this formula looks scary - just think of it as a fancy way of saying "add up the heights of all the hexagons up to the nth one." Finally, we come to the question at hand: what happens to $u\_n$ as we keep adding more and more hexagons? Does it stay the same, go to zero, or become infinitely large? Well, based on some calculations and estimates, it seems likely that $u\_n$ becomes larger and larger as $n$ gets bigger. However, nobody has been able to prove this yet! So if you're good at math and enjoy solving challenging problems, maybe you could be the one to figure it out. Good luck! Today, as a change from lists, my blog covers some of ways children benefit when adults guide their reading and read aloud to them, or they themselves read aloud. The results of several studies suggest that children's reading fluency and comprehension improve when adults read stories aloud to them. Even when studies fail to show a direct link between the two, the evidence strongly suggests that reading aloud, when combined with the child's reading lessons at school, can make a contribution to that child's reading proficiency. Studies have shown an even stronger result when parents and teachers encourage children to read a story aloud and provide guidance. Here are some suggestions I've gathered from around the Internet that you can use to guide your child's reading: - Ask your child to guess what a new book is about from looking at its cover and title. - Explain some of the bigger words in the story. - Talk about words that have multiple meanings. - Ask your child to point to the words while reading. - Encourage your child to spell out words and combine sounds. - Allow your child time to read difficult words. - Don't interrupt as the child reads. - Offer clues and encouragement when the child falters. - Encourage your child to repeat difficult words and sentences. - Ask your child to tell you in his/her own words what a difficult sentence or phrase means. - Discuss the story while reading and afterward. - Encourage your child to ask questions about the book while reading and afterward. - Ask your child to summarize the story in his/her own words. - Praise the child's efforts. - Be patient. - A few days after your child reads a new book, ask him or her to "remind" you of what the story was about. If your child is to become an enthusiastic, and therefore proficient, reader, reading must be fun. Here are a few suggestions for making it fun: - Ask your child to suggest a book, but be sure it's not too difficult. It's better to choose an easy book than one that will risk turning the reading session into a chore. The key here is that the reading material must be something your child wants to read, not what you think your child should read. - If your child suggests a comic book, welcome the choice. The highly visual medium of the comic book can lure children into reading, especially boys, who are traditionally more reluctant than girls to pick up a book. - If your child can't think of a book, start with books that relate to your child's hobbies and interests but of course make sure they're not too difficult. - Be sure to convey your own enthusiasm for reading the book. - Don't set unrealistic goals. - Be sure your child has a quiet spot where you both can read together undisturbed. Your child's room at bedtime is often the best choice. - Keep the reading time fairly short, maybe only ten minutes for a very young child. - Try substituting your child's name for a character in the story. - If your child has a favorite book, let him or her read it to you as often as he or she wants, even if you don't want to hear it again. - Read passages of the story yourself, so your child doesn't get tired and - Read slowly so your child has time to absorb the meaning. - Read using different voices for various characters; have fun with sound effects. - Encourage your child to use different voices too. - Act out some parts of the story with the child. - When traveling, have fun reading road signs and signs on storefronts. - Visit bookstores. - Try to find activities that connect with books your child has read. For example, visits to a natural history museum and zoo would tie in with books on animals. - Talk about books you enjoyed as a child and read them aloud if your child wants to you to. - Play word games with your child, including rhyming and syllable games. - Play board games that involve reading. Other things you can do to motivate your child to read: - Try to set aside a specific time every day for reading. - Don't make the schedule feel burdensome. - Take your child to the library regularly. Encourage book borrowing and attending story time at the children's library. - Ask friends and relatives to give your child books rather than toys. - Keep a variety of reading materials around your house from books and to brochures. - Make sure your child sees you reading for pleasure. - Encourage older children to read to younger ones. The older ones may enjoy showing off their skills. Now LE' me see you write it. Did you finish did you finish your bottle again? One of the major issues intersex people face is other people who are not intersex making them feel uncomfortable about their sex and gender. Intersex people may not feel the need to identify themselves at all in their lifetimes, preferring to maintain ambiguity. However, those who are not a part of this community feel as though people need to chose and be more clear about their genitalia. This invasion of privacy is often protested against and discussed by activist groups who believe intersex people are allowed to be private and deserve to be treated the same as those who are not intersex. Harpsichords are keyboard instruments that make sound by having the strings plucked with a plectrum. They are thought to have originated when a keyboard was attached to a psaltery. This makes them different from a clavichord where the strings are hit. The piano is therefore closer to a clavichord than a harpsichord. On a piano it is possible to play louder or quieter by playing the keys with more or less force. On a harpsichord the volume (playing louder or softer) cannot be controlled by the way it is played. However, some large harpsichords have several "stops", each one giving a different kind of sound. The largest harpsichords have two manuals (keyboards) which gives more variety. It makes it possible for the right hand to play the tune on one manual while the left hand plays an accompaniment more quietly on the other. what's all this about Santa Claus? Title: How to Friendzone a Guy: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: The friendzone is a concept that refers to a situation where one person in a friendship wishes to remain just friends while the other person has romantic feelings. While some people view being placed in the friendzone as undesirable, others may intentionally seek this type of relationship due to various reasons such as wanting emotional intimacy without romance or wishing to avoid commitment. This guide will provide you with detailed steps on how to friendzone a guy, ensuring clear communication, respect, and mutual understanding throughout the process. Step 1: Understand Your Feelings Before attempting to friendzone someone, take time to reflect on your emotions and motivations. Be honest about whether you genuinely want a platonic relationship or if you are simply avoiding confrontation regarding your true feelings. If you decide that a friendship is what you truly desire, proceed to the next step. Key tip: Avoid leading someone on by giving mixed signals; be upfront about your intentions from the beginning. Step 2: Establish Clear Boundaries Set firm boundaries early in the relationship to prevent any misunderstandings. Communicate openly about your expectations and limitations, making sure he understands that you value his company but do not have romantic interest. Examples include stating that you prefer group activities over one-on-one dates or clarifying that cuddling or holding hands crosses a line for you. Guideline: Consistency is crucial when enforcing boundaries. By maintaining these limits consistently, you can help ensure that the message remains clear. Step 3: Encourage Him To Pursue Other Interests Gently nudge him towards exploring new hobbies and meeting new people outside of your shared interests. Suggest joining clubs, attending social events, or participating in sports teams where he can meet potential partners. Key tip: Offer sincere compliments and support for his efforts in pursuing new endeavors. This demonstrates genuine care for his wellbeing and happiness beyond your own desires. Step 4: Maintain Platonic Affection Levels Refrain from engaging in behaviors typically associated with romantic relationships, such as excessive flirting, physical touch (beyond friendly hugs), or discussing intimate details of your personal lives. Instead, focus on fostering a strong friendship built upon trust, openness, and mutual respect. Guideline: Keep conversations lighthearted and fun rather than delving into deep emotional territory which could lead to misinterpretation. Step 5: Introduce Him To Potential Partners If appropriate, introduce him to single friends who share similar values and interests. Facilitating connections between him and other eligible bachelors allows him to expand his social circle and potentially find love elsewhere. Key tip: Refrain from setting unrealistic expectations or pressuring him into dating someone specific; instead, let organic chemistry develop naturally. Step 6: Respect His Reactions And Emotions Understand that transitioning into a friendship might be difficult for him initially, especially if he had stronger feelings for you. Show empathy and patience during this adjustment period, reassuring him that your bond remains valuable despite its non-romantic nature. Guideline: Continuously reaffirm your appreciation for him as a friend and emphasize the importance of maintaining a healthy, fulfilling connection moving forward. Conclusion: By following these steps, you can successfully navigate the complexities of friendzoning a guy while preserving mutual respect and fostering a meaningful platonic relationship. Remember always to prioritize open communication, maintain consistent boundaries, and demonstrate kindness and consideration throughout the entire process. yeah, right so you're not too pink. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV7/ET/jeaet7.cha = = = Anytime I go over to Joey's I can always wear his G I Joe pants. The Tempest Story for Kids: Some Monster of the Isle, Ebook Extract A storm was brewing and thunder rumbled in the distance. Caliban staggered under the burden of the wood he had collected but he had to keep going or his master's spirits would pinch him or frighten him with goblins, or throw him in the mud or lead him astray in the shape of a will-o'-the-wisp. He had to be careful because his master would set the spirits on him for every little thing. Sometimes they looked like apes, and made faces and chattered at him, and then bit him. At other times they looked like hedgehogs that lay tumbled up in his path as he walked barefoot, and raised their spines when he trod on them. Sometimes snakes were twined around him, hissing at him with their forked tongues, until he was driven mad. He pulled his ragged cloak tightly around him as a large thunder crack, very close, seemed to move the earth. And here was one of Prospero's spirits now, come to torment him for bringing the wood in too slowly. It was a most weird-looking spirit; half red, half yellow, and wearing a cap with bells on it. Perhaps if he fell flat on the ground the spirit wouldn't notice him. The 'spirit' was, in fact, Trinculo, the king's jester. Bewildered by his survival and marooned in this unfriendly place, all alone, he stumbled forward, worried about this alarming storm that was about to break. There wasn't a bush or a shrub here to keep off the weather at all. That black cloud - the huge one - looked like a foul leather bottle that was about to split open and spill its water. If the storm was going to be like the one that wrecked the ship he didn't know where he was going to hide his head. That cloud was going to rain down in bucketsful! He saw what looked like a filthy pile of rags spread on the ground and he stooped and lifted a corner of it. Something moved. He jabbed it with his toe and it moved again, more decisively this time. What did we have here? Was it a man or a fish? Dead or alive? Trinculo stooped and sniffed, and immediately recoiled. Phoo! A fish. It smelt like a fish, a very old and fish-like smell. A kind of not-so-fresh cheap, dried fish. A strange fish! He bent over and took a closer look at it. Hmm. It had legs like a man. And its fins were like arms. He touched one of them and sprang back. Warm! His opinion had changed. This was no fish, but an islander who had been struck by lightning. There was a mighty thundercrash. Trinculo looked about desperately. Hisbest bet was to creep under the monster's cloak; there wasn't any shelter around here. He hurried beneath the cloak and lay on top of the still figure, his legs pointing in the opposite direction from the creature's, and pulled the cloak over him. He would stay there until the storm was past. The ship's butler had also survived, although the miracle of it wasn't something he had been thinking about. He had surfed to shore on a barrel of wine and when he'd been cast up on the island he had made himself a bottle out of the bark of a tree and filled it from the barrel. And now he was quite drunk. He walked along, swaying, not a care in the world, singing: 'I shall no more to sea, to sea; here shall I die, ashore ..' He stopped. 'This is a very depressing song to sing at a time like this,' he said. 'Well, here's my comfort.' He took a swig and began singing a more cheerful song. The master, the swabber, the boatswain and I, The gunner and his mate. Lov'd Mall, Meg, and Marian and Margery; But none of us cared for Kate; For she had a tongue with a tang, Would cry to a sailor, Go hang! She loved not the savour of tar nor of pitch ' 'This is the wrong song too: but here's my comfort.' He took another swig but almost choked as a mournful voice came from the ground near him. 'Don't torment me!' Stephano looked down and saw the heap of old sacks. He prodded it with his foot. 'Ohhhhh!' came the voice again. Stephano crouched down and examined the mound. Two feet stuck out of one end. He looked for the head at the other end and saw that there were two feet sticking out there as well. He stood up and scratched his head. 'What's this?' he said. 'Have we got devils here? Are you playing tricks on me like in a freak show hey? I haven't escaped drowning to be scared, now, by your four legs, and I'm not going to run away.' Caliban's voice came again: 'The spirit is tormenting me! Ohhhhh!' Stephano swayed and pointed at the thing with his bottle. 'This is some four-legged monster of the isle who has, I think, a fever. Where the devil did he learn our language? I'll give him some medicine if that's the problem. If I can make him better and keep him tame, and get to Naples with him, he's a present for any emperor who ever wore expensive shoes.' 'Please don't torment me,' wailed Caliban. 'I'll bring my wood home faster.' 'He's in a fever now and talking nonsense,' said Stephano. 'I'll give him a taste of my bottle. If he's never drunk wine before it will help subdue his fever. If I can recover him and keep him docile the sky's the limit. Whoever buys him will pay a lot for him' Trinculo moved now and Caliban moaned. 'You haven't hurt me yet but I know you will. I know it by your trembling. Prospero is controlling you.' Stephano lifted the cloak: Caliban's terrified eyes stared up at him. 'Come on,' said Stephano, 'open your mouth. Here's something that will make you talk. Open your mouth!' Caliban obediently opened his mouth and Stephano stuck the bottleneck in it. 'This will shake off your shaking, I can tell you,' he said. Caliban sucked on the bottleneck and coughed as the wine hit his throat. Stephano laughed and removed the bottle. When Caliban's convulsions had subsided he offered the bottle once more. 'Come on, open your mouth again,' he said. Trinculo raised his head. 'I know that voice,' he said in a small, frightened voice. 'It's . but he's drowned. And these are devils. Oh! Help!' Stephano fell over then struggled to his feet again. 'Four legs and two voices!' he exclaimed. 'A very clever monster! If it takes all the wine in my bottle to help him I'll do it. Come. Amen. I'll pour some into your other mouth.' He lifted the cloak. Trinculo saw him and uttered his relief 'Stephano!' Stephano dropped the cloak and turned to run. 'Is your other mouth calling me by name? Mercy! Mercy! This is a devil, not a monster. I'm off. I don't have a long spoon!' He started running. Trinculo called after him 'Stephano! If you're Stephano speak to me. I'm Trinculo. Don't be afraid. Your good friend, Trinculo!' Stephano stopped and came back cautiously. 'If you're Trinculo, come out. I'll pull you by the smaller legs.' He examined the legs and saw the jester's motley. 'If any are Trinculo's legs these are the ones.' He whipped the cloak away. Trinculo rolled off Caliban then stood up. 'You are Trinculo!' He embraced his friend. Caliban was paralysed in his confusion, and lay still, watching the two newcomers. He felt light headed. It was a good feeling. He licked his lips and tasted the delicious wine that had caused it. 'I thought he had been killed by the lightning,' said Trinculo. 'But weren't you drowned, Stephano? I hope you aren't drowned. Has the storm blown itself out? I hid under the dead monster's cloak in fear of the storm.' He grasped Stephano and began dancing wildly with him. 'Are you really living, Stephano? Oh, Stephano. Two of us escaped!' 'Please!' exclaimed Stephano, pulling himself away from his delighted friend. 'Don't turn me around like that; my stomach is unsettled.' " Thomas the Nestorian had learned two valuable lessons in his much wandering about the earth,--never to appear surprised, and always to be ready to act quickly. So, knowing nothing of the possible results of his action, but feeling that it could scarcely be worse than death from Tartar spears, he leaped back, as bidden. The next instant, he found himself flat upon his back in one of the low-ceiled cliff caves that abound in Western China, while the screen of vines that had concealed its entrance still quivered from his fall. Picking himself up and breathing a prayer of thanks for his deliverance, he peered through the leafy doorway and beheld in surprise six much astonished Tartar robbers regarding with looks of puzzled wonder a defiant little Chinese girl, who had evidently darted out of the cave as he had tumbled in. It is often used for making models of cars, boats, helicopters and airplanes move on their own like real ones. For controlling models, an operator uses a transmitter that has the model's controls on it. When a control is moved, the radio signal changes. The part on the model that takes the changing signals and helps turn them into movement is called the receiver. The receiver then tells a special kind of motor called a servo to move part of the model. He did not notice, but went his round of exploration. Dad that is a circle like a clock? There are some simple strategies for writing jokes that just can't lose. Let's start out with jokes that are based on a popular expression. Every kid has heard some adult say: "He's got ants in his pants." Or (just to be fair) "She's got ants in her pants." It's funny to imagine someone jumping around because he (or she) has ants in his (or her) pants. That's why it could be the punch line for a great joke. Is there an animal that really could have ants swarming all over its body? You bet! An aardvark breaks into anthills and chows down on the insects inside. As ants rush to safety, some scurry up the aardvark's long snout. It's a good thing an aardvark can shut its nostrils tight! Now that you have an animal in mind, you need to think of a question that hints at the answer. Let's try this one: Q: Why did the aardvark jump for joy? A: It had ants in its pants. When kids study the solar system, they learn that life on our planet couldn't exist without a steady stream of light rays from the Sun. Here's how you can use that science fact and a popular expression to write a great joke: Q: What did Earth say to the Sun? A: You're the light of my life! Okay, okay. That joke might have gotten a groan instead of a laugh. After all, it's a little bit sappy. Next, let's try a joke that's more gross and goofy. When someone isn't brave enough to do something, we say he or she "has no guts." You can turn that expression into a joke that includes something every kid knows about the human body: Q: Why didn't the skeleton cross the road. A: It had no guts. Hope that one made you chuckle. Now it's your turn. Can you or the kids you know come up with jokes that use the following expressions as punch lines? earth science joke: "It was your fault." animal joke: "It has two left feet." space joke: "You're hot stuff!" Here's a book that might help: Terban, Marvin. Scholastic Dictionary of Idioms. New York: Scholastic, 2006. An idiom is an expression that can't be understood based on the meanings of the individual words. Examples include kicked the bucket, which means "died" or keep tabs on, which means "watch closely." Feel free to post your best jokes in the comments. We could all use a good laugh. Be on the lookout for more joke-writing posts in the future. And check out the Super Silly Science Jokes I post on Friday. 'Cause that's where you go when you die. I said this is kind of pretty. What can you put on the plate? Title: How to Be a Female Vampire on www.PixieHollow.com Pixie Hollow is a popular online role-playing game where players can create their own characters and interact with others in a magical world. One of the many character options available is that of a female vampire. Here's a comprehensive guide on how to become one: **Step 1: Access Pixie Hollow** Firstly, visit the official website of Pixie Hollow at [www.pixiehollow.com](http://www.pixiehollow.com). Click on the "Play Now" button to begin. If you are new to the site, you will need to register an account by providing some basic information such as your date of birth (which must show you are over 13 years old), username, password, and email address. Once registered, log into your newly created account. **Step 2: Navigate to Character Creation** After logging in, click on the 'Create A New Friend' option which takes you to the character creation screen. This is where you start building your female vampire character. **Step 3: Choose Gender** Select 'Female' under the gender section. Remember, this guide focuses on creating a female vampire so this selection is crucial. **Step 4: Select Species** Choose 'Vampire' from the list of species. Unfortunately, unlike other games, Pixie Hollow does not offer different types of vampires like 'Daywalker' or 'Ancient'. However, don't worry; you still have plenty of customization options ahead! **Step 5: Customize Your Appearance** Now comes the fun part - designing your female vampire's look! You can choose her skin tone, eye color, hair style & color, clothing, wings, and more. While these choices do not affect gameplay, they allow you to express your creativity and personalize your character. **Key Tips**: 1. Although traditional vampire lore suggests pale complexions, feel free to select any skin tone you prefer for your character. 2. For a classic vampire appearance, consider choosing dark colors for eyes and hair. 3. There aren't many outfits explicitly designed for vampires, but pick something that fits the mysterious and nocturnal nature of vampires, perhaps blacks, purples, or deep reds. Push it down really hard and it will click. Okay where's the other one at? Wood fires can usually be put out with water because water absorbs heat, but metal fires are too hot for water to absorb enough heat to put out the fire. The two emphatic tenses receive their name because they are used for emphasis. More commonly, however, they are used with the negative not and with questions when the normal order is inverted and part of the verb comes before the subject. The present emphatic tense is formed by adding the basic present form of the verb to the present tense of the verb to do (do or does). The past emphatic tense is formed by adding the basic present form of the verb to the past tense of the verb to do (did). Present emphatic: Does he run fast? He does run fast. He does not run slowly. Past emphatic: He did come to work today. Didn't he stay home? He did not stay home today. [to Bro has he picks up bag of crackers] Do you think that would make a good table? [6 second pause while Chi looks to see where the piece fits] Okay then use the blue stop lights. Hello young scholars! Today, we're going to learn about something called " tale coverings." Now, don't get scared by the big name - it's just a fancy way of saying "a bunch of maps that fit together nicely." Imagine you have a big map of a country, and on this map, there are lots of smaller maps showing different regions or cities. Each of these small maps covers a part of the big map, meaning that if you put them all together, they would completely cover the entire country. This is similar to what happens with tale coverings! In mathematics, when we talk about schemes, we can think of them like those maps. An tale covering is like having many overlapping maps (or mathematical spaces) that perfectly cover another single map (or mathematical space). The crucial thing here is that each of these little maps must be " tale," which means they look pretty much like the original map near every point, but they could be rotated, flipped, or stretched without distortion. You can imagine it as looking at a tiny portion of your map through a magnifying glass; everything looks the same, but maybe upside down or mirrored. The beauty of tale coverings lies in their properties. First, each map needs to be "flat," so nothing gets squished or stretched out of shape while putting everything together. Secondly, the collection of small maps should cover the whole area - no gaps allowed! And finally, given any region on the large map, there should be only finitely many ways to cut it into pieces using boundaries from the little maps. In other words, things need to stay manageable and not become infinitely complicated. So next time you play with puzzles made up of interlocking shapes, remember that mathematicians study similar ideas when working with tale coverings! yeah, that gives you somewhere to go from. It takes years of practice to become a good violinist. A beginner will start with pieces and or exercises that do not require precise or complicated technique in right or left hand. Some examples of pieces that do not require great technique are "Twinkle Twinkle Little Star" by Mozart, "Children's Song" (A German folk tune), and "Over the Rainbow". During these "simple" songs, the violinist will develop fundamental skills necessary for all other techniques, such as proper bow and violin holding. As the musician develops more and more confidence and skill in both left and right hand, pieces and exercises will become progressively more difficult. When necessary, they will also learn techniques and skills that will enhance their playing. Vibrato, smooth bow changes in the right hand, and shifting. An' you had ta get it caught in the gear. In his "Philosophical Investigations", Wittgenstein demonstrated that the elements (parts) of games, such as play, rules, and competition, all fail to correctly define what games are. He concluded that people apply the term "game" to a range of different human activities that are related, but not closely related. Becoming an effective teammate in Halo 3 requires a combination of individual skill, communication, strategy, and adaptability. Here's a comprehensive guide with detailed steps and helpful tips: **Step 1: Master Basic Controls and Mechanics** Before you can be an effective teammate, ensure that you have mastered the fundamental controls and mechanics of Halo 3. This includes understanding movement (strafe-jumping, thrust-jumps), weapon management, grenade usage, equipment handling, as well as aiming and shooting techniques. Familiarize yourself with the map layouts and callouts to efficiently communicate with your teammates. Being skilled at these basics will help establish trust within your team, making them more likely to rely on you during crucial moments. **Step 2: Choose Your Role Wisely** Depending on the game mode, different roles are necessary for success. For example, in objective-based modes like Capture The Flag or Assault, having dedicated players for offense, defense, and support is essential. In Slayer matches, focus on being a consistent damage dealer while still providing assistance when needed. Adapt to what your team lacks; if they need a sniper, switch to one even if it isn't your preferred playstyle. **Step 3: Communicate Regularly** Effective communication is vital in any team-oriented game. Constantly update your team about enemy positions, movements, and strategies using clear, concise language. Utilize voice chat whenever possible, but text-based communications also work effectively. Remember to remain positive and avoid blaming others for mistakes - this fosters a supportive environment where everyone feels comfortable sharing information. **Step 4: Coordinate With Your Team** Work together with your team to develop strategies based on your collective strengths and weaknesses. Discuss loadouts, tactics, and potential problem areas before starting a match. During the game, stick close to your allies to provide backup and cover fire. When attacking or defending objectives, move as a unit rather than individually. Coordinated attacks increase the chances of success significantly. **Step 5: Support Your Teammates** Always look out for opportunities to assist your comrades. Revive downed teammates promptly, share power weapons and ammo, suppress enemies to allow safe passage, and create distractions when necessary. These small acts contribute greatly towards overall victory. **Step 6: Stay Alert and Aware** Pay attention to your surroundings and stay vigilant for threats. Keep track of both friendly and hostile radar blips to anticipate engagements. Learn to recognize common camping spots and high traffic zones. Maintaining situational awareness ensures quicker reactions and better decision-making under pressure. **Step 7: Practice Good Sportsmanship** Maintain respectful behavior towards all players, regardless of their performance or attitude. Avoid trash talking, quitters, or those who intentionally sabotage their own teams. By acting graciously in defeat and humbly in victory, you set a good example for other community members and promote a healthier gaming atmosphere. **Step 8: Analyze Matches Post-Game** After every match, review what went right and wrong. Identify instances where better coordination could have led to success or prevented failure. Use this knowledge to improve future performances and strengthen team dynamics. By following these steps, you'll become a valuable asset to any Halo 3 team, enhancing not only your personal experience but contributing positively to the entire community. Title: Build Up Your Speed as a Pace Bowler in Cricket Introduction: Becoming a fast bowler in cricket requires dedication, hard work, and proper technique. It's not just about raw strength or power; it also involves understanding the mechanics of your body and the physics of ball delivery. This tutorial will guide you through various steps to help increase your pace, improve accuracy, and reduce injury risks. Step 1: Understand the Basics Before starting any training program, understand the basics of fast bowling. The action generally consists of four phases - run-up, leap, landing, and delivery stride. Each phase plays a crucial role in maintaining balance, generating speed, and directing energy towards the batsman. * Run-up: This is where you generate momentum. A longer run-up doesn't necessarily mean faster bowling. The aim should be to reach optimal speed while maintaining control. * Leap: Also known as the 'bound', this helps transfer horizontal speed into vertical lift. Proper timing and height are essential here. * Landing: This decides your direction and alignment. Your leading foot must land parallel to the crease. * Delivery Stride: The final phase where you release the ball. Power comes from rotation of hips and shoulders along with extension of the bowling arm. Step 2: Improve Fitness Levels Cricket isn't only about upper body strength. Lower body fitness contributes significantly to bowling speed. Work on improving cardiovascular endurance, leg strength, flexibility, and agility. Regular exercises such as running, swimming, cycling, squats, lunges, and plyometrics can enhance these areas. Key Tip: Always warm up before workouts and cool down afterwards to prevent injuries. Step 3: Focus on Core Strength A strong core allows better transmission of force from lower to upper body during bowling. Exercises like planks, Russian twists, bicycle crunches, and medicine ball throws can strengthen your abdominal muscles, obliques, and lower back. Guideline: Remember to maintain correct form during all exercises to avoid strain or injury. Step 4: Perfect Your Bowling Action An efficient bowling action minimizes energy loss and maximizes speed. Work closely with a coach to ensure your technique is correct. Common faults include dropping the front shoulder, crossing the line at delivery, or poor hip rotation. Key Tip: Film yourself bowling to analyze your action and identify potential improvements. Step 5: Train Specifically for Fast Bowling Incorporate specific drills into your practice sessions. For example, mark out cones for your run-up and focus on reaching top speed by the time you reach the crease. Use weighted balls or resistance bands to build power. Guideline: Gradually decrease the use of weights or resistances to allow your body to adapt to increased speeds without risking injury. Step 6: Nutrition and Recovery Eating balanced meals rich in proteins, complex carbohydrates, and healthy fats provides necessary nutrients for muscle repair and growth. Stay hydrated throughout the day and especially before, during, and after training. Allow adequate rest between intense training sessions to facilitate recovery. Key Tip: Consider consulting a sports nutritionist for personalized advice. Remember, building speed takes time and patience. Don't rush the process; instead, concentrate on perfecting your technique and overall fitness level. With consistent effort and guidance, you can become a quicker, more effective pace bowler. Ma I'm taking the water out of the bathtub. What would you do with two moms? This lesson has several references to inversion geometry. If you are unfamiliar with the subject, you may wish to refer to this guide: Inversion Geometry This is a geometry challenge that might seem to be simplistic: We want a mechanism that will draw a straight line segment. Your first impulse is to draw it with a straightedge, but that would not be fair. Somebody else had to make the straightedge straight. When you think about it, circles are easy. A compass can be made from any rigid object. Attach a pin to one end of a canoe paddle, and attach a pencil to the other. The paddle will draw a perfect circle. But using a straightedge requires a leap of faith. We cannot be certain that it is straight. In 1864, the problem was solved by Captain Charles-Nicolas Peucellier, a French artillery officer. Several other linkages followed. Some were simpler, but they all seem to be variations of the Peucellier linkage. In 1877, they were summarized in How to Draw a Straight Line: A Lecture on Linkages, by Alfred Bray Kempe. The book was reprinted by the National Council of Teachers of Mathematics in 1977. All of the linkages presented here are from that book. Peucellier approached the problem with inversion geometry. He knew that the inverse of a circle through the center of inversion is a line. It is easy enough to make a point follow a circle, so he only needed to design a linkage that would invert that point. The image below is called the Peucellier cell. All of the line segments represent rigid links, like colors are congruent, and the intersection points are hinges. It will be shown that if point A is fixed and point P moves freely (as far as the links will allow it), then point Q is the inverse of point P. The center of inversion is A. The power of inversion, r2, will be derived in the proof. For the purpose of this proof, let it be given that AB > PB. That is to say that the blue links are longer than the green links. It must be shown that if P moves while A is fixed, the product (AP)(AQ) is equal to some constant r2. It is clear from the symmetry that points A, P, and Q are collinear. Let point R be the intersection of the diagonals of quadrilateral PBQC. Since it is a rhombus, the diagonals must be perpendicular. The values AB and PB are measures of rigid links, and therefore are constant. This means that the product (AP)(AQ) is constant. Let r2 = (AB)2 - (PB)2. Do you see now why we needed the condition AB > PB? Actually, the linkage will still work, even if the green links are longer than the blue. The linkage would work, but the proof would not. In that case, points P and Q would not be inverses of each other. There would be another transformation between them. See if you can work it out. The only thing left is to force point P into the path of a circle through A. The linkage below should do the job. The small circles represent fixed anchor points. Since they are fixed, the link between them is unnecessary, but it is there to show the geometric relationship. Notice that point P cannot trace the entire circle without breaking some of the links. That is why Q traces a line segment rather that an entire line. Click on the image to see a simulation. Below are some other linkages that draw straight line segments. It will be seen that they are actually variations on the Peucellier linkage. |It may look different, but this is the Peucellier linkage again. Geometrically, there is no reason why the rhombus links should have to be shorter than the others.|| |Using the lower anchor as the dilation point, dilate the lower part of the cell. That point at the bottom is the new pencil point. The locus of its pre-image was a line segment, so this point must trace the dilation of that line segment, which is simply another line segment.|| |Try another dilation. This time use a dilation ratio of -1. That causes the new pencil point to be on the other side of the anchor. Still it must trace a line segment.|| |Click on the image at right to see another java simulation.|| This version is the simplest mechanism, but it may be more difficult to understand. |Once more, begin with the Peucellier linkage.|| |Dilate four of the links about the upper anchor point. Point Q' is the new pencil point. Since its pre-image traced a line segment, this point will too. The two links on the right may now be discarded without compromising the mechanism.|| |Draw the line of symmetry of the original cell, and draw parallel lines through points B and B'. Two of the links are extended to the two outside lines. Notice that these two links intersect the parallel lines at the same angle, and are therefore congruent. In fact, they are the legs of an isosceles trapezoid with diagonal BB'. Point Q' is still the pencil point.|| |Insert another link as the other diagonal of the trapezoid. This will support the shape of the mechanism, so some of the other links now may be removed.|| |Of all of the solutions, this appears to be the most economical. Click on the image to view a simulation.|| Back to Whistler Alley Mathematics Um we don't have big wooden ones like this. Why don't you scoot out there, and I'll come out in a minute okay? You might have heard your teacher or older student mention them before - don't worry, they're not as complicated as they sound! Think of a matrix like a grid or table with rows and columns. Each little box where a row and column intersect is called an element. Let's take a look at this example: [3 5; 7 9] This is a 2x2 matrix because it has 2 rows and 2 columns. It has 4 elements: one in the top left, top right, bottom left, and bottom right. Can you count them all? Now, let's say someone asked you how many elements there would be in a bigger 4x4 matrix. Well, since each row has 4 boxes and there are 4 rows, you just multiply those numbers together! That means a 4x4 matrix would have 16 elements. Sometimes people will ask you for specific elements in a matrix. To find these, simply use the row and column numbers to locate the correct spot. For example, if I wanted the element in the second row and third column of a 4x4 matrix, I would go down two rows and over three columns from the top left corner. Lastly, did you know that even young kids can use matrices without realizing it? Imagine trying to organize toys into different categories on a shelf. Maybe you want to put all the cars in one row and dolls in another row, while keeping everything organized by color within those rows. By setting up your toys like this, you've actually created a real-life matrix! Just like solving puzzles or organizing things, learning about matrices can be fun and helpful in understanding more complex concepts later on. She attended Cowan School with her sister Charlotte. ||You don't need to memorize this diagram. But you do need to understand how to find the notes. To do this, you'll need to know two things: (1) the open tones of each string, and (2) the notes of the chromatic scale. (1). The open tones (the note that sounds when the string is unfretted) of the four strings are G, C, E, and A. This is the familiar "My Dog Has Fleas" melody that all ukulele players know. (2). Each time you move one fret higher on a string, you move one note higher on the chromatic scale. The twelve notes of the chromatic scale are A, A#/Bb, B, C, C#/Db, D, D#/Eb, E, F, F#/Gb, G, G#/Ab. Looking at the diagram, you can see how the chromatic scale extends up the fretboard, one note at a time, starting at the open tone for each If you're wondering why the chromatic scale doesn't always have a flat or sharp between each of the "natural" notes of the scale, take a look at the scale as it shows up on a piano keyboard. The white keys are the natural notes (A, B, C, ...) and the black keys are the sharps and flats. The irregular pattern of sharps and flats is represented in the irregular pattern of black keys on the piano. You can see that there are no sharps or flats between B and C, or between E and F. Title: How to Change the Size of an Icon on a Windows PC Windows operating system allows users to customize their desktop by changing the size of icons. This can be helpful when trying to fit more or fewer icons on your screen, making them easier to see, or just personalizing your computer's appearance. Here is a step-by-step guide on how to change the size of an icon on a Windows PC. **Step 1: Locate the Desktop Icons** The first step in changing the size of an icon involves locating where they are. By default, these appear as small pictures representing various applications, files, folders, etc., placed over the background image known as 'wallpaper'. You will find these icons on your desktop - the main screen that appears after logging into your account. **Step 2: Zoom In or Out Using Mouse Scroll Wheel** Hover your mouse cursor over any empty space on the desktop. Now, if you have a scroll wheel on your mouse, press down gently and roll it upwards (towards yourself) to increase the size of all desktop icons simultaneously. Conversely, rolling the wheel downward decreases the icon sizes. With this method, you don't need to know exactly which key combinations to use, and it provides immediate feedback since you can watch the icons grow larger or smaller right before your eyes. *Tip:* If you do not have a mouse with a scroll wheel, refer to Step 3 below. **Key Guideline:** The further you move the scroll wheel away from you, the bigger the icons become; moving the scroll wheel towards you makes the icons smaller. **Step 3: Use Keyboard Shortcuts** For those without a mouse with a scroll wheel, there are keyboard shortcuts available to resize icons. To enlarge icons, hold down the Ctrl key while pressing the plus sign (+) key. For reducing the icon size, keep holding the Ctrl key but tap the minus sign (-) key instead. Each time you hit either the + or - key, the icons will adjust accordingly. Release both keys once you reach the desired icon size. *Important Note:* Make sure no program windows are active when using these keystrokes. They should only affect the icons present on the desktop. **Step 4: Confirm Changes** After altering the icon sizes, take a moment to ensure everything looks good. It might help to navigate around different parts of the desktop to check whether any text within the icons becomes hard to read due to excessive scaling. Remember, you can always revert back to the original settings if necessary. **Step 5: Resetting Icon Sizes Back to Default** If at any point you wish to restore the default icon size, repeat Steps 2 or 3 until the icons return to their initial state. Alternatively, right-click anywhere on the desktop, select "View," then click "Medium icons" to quickly reset the view. In conclusion, changing the size of icons on a Windows PC is straightforward and quick. Whether using a mouse or keyboard, following our simple steps enables easy customization tailored to individual preferences. Enjoy exploring other features offered by your Windows OS! We see the Moon orbit the Earth, objects fall to the ground, and birds fly - all of these are because of forces. force is a push or a pull. We measure forces in "Newtons" Sir Isaac Newton (1642 1 Newton isn't a very big force: it's about the weight of an apple. the direction is important. - the speed of - the direction that an object is moving in - the shape of an object. is a force that acts towards the centre of the This means that, wherever you are in the world, "down" is always towards the ground - even though your "down" isn't the same direction as anybody else's. pull of the Earth is what gives objects weight. Thus weight is a force - it's how hard the Earth is pulling on an object. The Earth pulls on every kilogramme with a force of ten Newtons. We say that the Earth's gravitational field strength (at ground level) is 10 Newtons per kilogramme (10 N/kg) (Actually, it's more like 9.81, but for GCSE we usually call it 10.) In other words, an object with a mass of 1kg has a weight of 10N. is a very weak force, you need a very large mass in order to get a noticeable gravitational pull. An odd thing about gravity - it always attracts objects and never on the question to reveal the answers) How hard does the Earth's gravity pull on a 2kg object? What is the weight of a 4kg rock? What is the weight of a 100 gramme plastic toy? Isaac Newton came up with three "Laws of Motion" back in the 17th century, which we still find useful today. For the moment, we need:- says that if the forces on an object are in balance, the object's speed and direction of motion won't (In other words, if you leave it alone, it'll carry on doing whatever it was doing already.) the forces on an object are in balance, then the object's velocity is constant. This means that if it's not moving, it'll stay still; or if it is moving, it'll continue in a straight line at a constant speed . What if the forces aren't in balance? The combined effect of all the forces on an object is called the resultant force. GCSE, you'll usually only be asked to deal with forces which are all in a straight line. Forces are two-way affairs, they're about the way that objects affect each other. you're pushing down on your chair with a force of, maybe, 500N, and the chair is pushing back with a force of 500N. [the cookie starts to split in half] Title: How to Change the Language in Minecraft Minecraft is a popular sandbox game that allows players to build and explore virtual worlds made up of block-like structures. The game supports multiple languages, which can be changed according to your preference. This tutorial will guide you through changing the language settings in Minecraft. **Table of Contents:** 1. Launch Minecraft 2. Access Settings 3. Select Game Language 4. Confirm Changes 5. Key Tips and Guidelines **Step 1: Launch Minecraft** Firstly, open the application or platform where Minecraft is installed (such as the official launcher, Microsoft Store, or Steam). Click on its icon to initiate the process. Make sure you have administrative rights if using a computer. **Step 2: Access Settings** Once launched, click on 'Settings' from the main menu. It may also appear as a gear icon depending upon the version of Minecraft you are running. This action reveals various configuration options related to graphics, audio, controls, etc., including the one we need - language settings. **Step 3: Select Game Language** In the settings window, look for an option labeled 'Language', usually located under the 'Interface' tab. Upon clicking this dropdown list, you'll see several available languages. Choose your preferred language by clicking on it. Some common choices include English, Spanish, French, German, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Italian, Dutch, Russian, Portuguese, and many more. *Key Tip:* If you cannot find your desired language, check whether your game version supports it. You might require updating or reinstalling the game to gain access to additional languages. **Step 4: Confirm Changes** After selecting your preferred language, ensure all other settings are correct and then confirm changes by saving them. There should be a button like 'Apply' or 'Save Changes'. Once confirmed, exit the settings menu and return to the main screen; any text displayed within the game should now appear in your chosen language. *Key Guideline:* Remember, these steps change only the display language inside the game interface but do not affect multiplayer sessions with friends who speak different languages unless they too adjust their individual settings accordingly. By following these straightforward steps, you can enjoy playing Minecraft in your preferred language! Um so maybe 'cause it was more familiar to her but the other stuffed animals' no. Read to your Baby You are your baby's first teacher! From the moment a baby is born, they are ready to learn, and they begin to understand language long before they begin to speak. Very young children acquire knowledge through all of their senses - sight, sound, touch, taste and smell. Reading to your baby helps foster brain development and early literacy skills. The American Academy of Pediatrics has recommended that pediatricians prescribe reading activities to children at their well visits and strongly recommends reading daily to babies from six months of age. Here's what we know: Babies begin to learn about language from birth. Reading aloud exposes your child to the sounds of human speech. By the age of two, children know between 300-500 words. Children who are spoken to and frequently read to have larger vocabularies and develop into better readers. Sharing books with your baby lays the foundation for learning to read. Help your baby: - Attend Babytime at the Ferguson Library! - Allow your baby to explore board books or soft, vinyl books. Babies often learn about their world by chewing on things - sometimes reading looks like chewing! - Point to different things in the book and name them. - Your baby loves the sound of your voice, sing aloud and be proud. Nursery rhymes and songs are fun at any time - in the car, at bath time, at mealtime - and help develop language skills. - Use different voices when you read to your child. Give different characters different voices and make the sound of the animals that your baby sees. - Ask your baby questions about what's happening on the page, such as "Where is the cow?". Pause, then point and answer for the baby, "Oh, there's the cow!" - Have fun! Relax and enjoy this special time with your baby. If you're not in the mood, or your baby is cranky or distracted, then it's not the time to share a book. Babies Like Books with: - Photos of other babies! - Clear pictures of familiar things in their world. - Lots of rhythm and repetition. - Animals and animal sounds. - Textures! Touch-and-feel books are great. At Gloucester Cathedral the Perpendicular East Window is said to be as large as a tennis court. There are three very famous large chapels in this style- King's College Chapel, Cambridge; St. George's Chapel at Windsor Castle; Henry VII's Chapel at Westminster Abbey. The other famous example is Bath Abbey. Well that's just what the book says. Title: How to Fix a Chain Falling Off the Chainring Without Stopping Introduction: A chain falling off the chainring can be an annoying issue while cycling, especially if you're in the middle of your commute or a race. This tutorial will guide you through fixing this problem without having to stop your bike, ensuring minimal disruption to your ride. It is essential to learn this skill as it enhances safety, saves time, and boosts self-confidence during rides. Before starting, make sure that you are familiar with different components of the bicycle drivetrain and have basic maintenance skills. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Practice in a safe environment before attempting on busy roads or trails. * Keep both hands on the handlebars when performing these steps for better control and safety. * Use appropriate clothing and footwear to prevent injury from grease or moving parts. * Be aware of traffic conditions around you even as you perform these adjustments. Step 1: Identify the Problem While riding, notice any unusual noises such as clanking sounds coming from the drivetrain area, which might indicate a loose chain or one fallen off the chainring. You may also feel irregularities like slipping gears or difficulty pedaling smoothly. If either occurs, proceed to inspect the chain position closely. Step 2: Check Chain Position Without stopping completely, reduce speed by gently applying brakes. Shift into a lower gear using your shifters (this increases the space between the chainring teeth and the chain) making it easier to see whether the chain has slipped off. Look down at the cranks and observe if the chain is engaged correctly with all teeth on the largest front chainring. Step 3: Prepare for Adjustment Once confirmed that the chain indeed fell off, prepare yourself mentally and physically for the next steps. Remember, maintaining balance and controlling the bike is crucial here; hence keeping both hands firmly gripped onto the handlebar is vital. Lean slightly towards the side where the chain needs repositioning to counterbalance the weight shift. Step 4: Reposition the Chain Using your right hand (for most bikes), reach down near the bottom bracket close to the dropped chain. Gently lift the chain and place it back onto the nearest tooth on the chainring, ensuring that it engages securely into the groove. Avoid forcing the chain over multiple teeth at once, as doing so could lead to further issues or damage. Step 5: Secure the Chain After successfully placing the chain onto a single tooth, continue guiding it along the remaining teeth until fully secured onto the chainring again. As you do this, gradually increase your pedal rotation to help ease the process. Once confident that the chain is properly seated, start pedaling normally to ensure smooth operation. Step 6: Confirm Proper Functionality Lastly, confirm that everything works fine by shifting through all available gears. Listen carefully for abnormal noises, pay attention to pedaling resistance, and monitor overall performance. Should you detect any problems, consider stopping safely to investigate further or seek professional assistance if necessary. Conclusion: Congratulations! By following these steps, you have now learned how to fix a chain falling off the chainring without stopping - a valuable skill every cyclist should possess. Regular maintenance checks and practice will improve proficiency, enabling quicker response times in real-world situations. Happy Riding! Translated from the German By FERNANDA SAVAGE Contents INTRODUCTION VENUS IN FURS INTRODUCTION Leopold von Sacher-Masoch was born in Lemberg, Austrian Galicia, on January 27, 1836. He studied jurisprudence at Prague and Graz, and in 1857 became a teacher at the latter university. He published several historical works, but soon gave up his academic career to devote himself wholly to literature. Title: How to Change the Order of Filters on Instagram Instagram offers a variety of filters that allow you to enhance your photos before sharing them with your followers. The order of these filters can be customized according to your preference. This tutorial will guide you through changing the order of filters on Instagram. **Step 1:** Open Your Instagram App Start by opening the Instagram app on your mobile device. If you're not already logged in, enter your username and password to proceed. **Step 2:** Navigate to Camera or Gallery Screen Tap on the '+' icon at the bottom center part of the screen which takes you either directly to the camera if you want to take a new photo, or to your gallery where you can select an existing picture to edit. **Step 3:** Select a Photo If you chose to go to the gallery, browse through your pictures until you find one you wish to filter. Once selected, tap on it to open it in the editing mode. **Step 4:** Preview Filter Options Swipe right across the filter options available below your image preview. As you swipe, notice how each filter changes the look of your photo so you have an idea about what kind of effect they create. **Step 5:** Hold Down on a Filter Icon Now here comes the tricky yet interesting part - rearranging the filters! To do this, press down firmly (or hold down) on any filter icon until all icons start shaking subtly. When this happens, you know you are in "edit mode". **Key Tip**: Don't worry when your photo starts shaking along with the filters; it's normal because we've entered into edit mode. **Step 6:** Drag & Drop Filters With the filter icons still wobbling slightly, grab onto the filter you'd like to move. You do this by placing your finger gently on the left side of the filter icon until it lifts up slightly then dragging it wherever you desire within the row of filters. **Guideline**: Make sure there is no gap between the moved filter and its neighbors. A tiny line should connect them indicating continuation without spaces. **Step 7:** Set New Filter Order Keep moving filters around until you reach a sequence that suits your taste. Remember, the first filter shown during regular browsing will always be the last one you placed while arranging. **Step 8:** Exit Edit Mode Once satisfied with your newly arranged filter set, tap anywhere outside the filter area to exit edit mode. All elements including your edited photo stop shaking. **Step 9:** Apply Changes After exiting edit mode, don't forget to hit 'Next' located at the top-right corner. Review other settings such as caption, location tag, etc., then share your masterpiece by tapping 'Share'. Congratulations! You now understand how to change the order of filters on Instagram, giving yourself quicker access to your favorite ones. Enjoy experimenting with different sequences and discovering new ways to express yourself visually. Can I go to the moon asked baby bear?
